err == nil {\n\t\t\t\tve.Column = col\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Extract contextual information\n\tlines := strings.Split(content, \"\\n\")\n\tif ve.Line > 0 && ve.Line <= len(lines) {\n\t\t// Show the problematic line\n\t\tve.Context = fmt.Sprintf(\"Line content: %q\", lines[ve.Line-1])\n\n\t\t// If we have a column, show a pointer to the error\n\t\tif ve.Column > 0 && ve.Column <= len(lines[ve.Line-1]) {\n\t\t\tpointer := strings.Repeat(\" \", ve.Column-1) + \"^\"\n\t\t\tve.Context += fmt.Sprintf(\"\\n             %s\", pointer)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Categorize the error based on message content\n\tve.Type = categorizeError(errMsg)\n\n\treturn ve\n}\n\n// categorizeError determines the error type based on the error message.\nfunc categorizeError(errMsg string) ErrorType {\n\tlower := strings.ToLower(errMsg)\n\n\t// Check for specific error patterns\n\tswitch {\n\tcase strings.Contains(lower, \"could not find expected\"):\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeSyntax\n\tcase strings.Contains(lower, \"did not find expected key\"):\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeSyntax\n\tcase strings.Contains(lower, \"found character that cannot start any key\"):\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeSyntax\n\tcase strings.Contains(lower, \"invalid indentation\"):\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeSyntax\n\tcase strings.Contains(lower, \"duplicate key\"):\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeStructure\n\tcase strings.Contains(lower, \"mapping values are not allowed\"):\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeSyntax\n\tcase strings.Contains(lower, \"unexpected end\"):\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeSyntax\n\tcase strings.Contains(lower, \"unacceptable character\"):\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeSyntax\n\tcase strings.Contains(lower, \"scanner error\"):\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeSyntax\n\tcase strings.Contains(lower, \"unmarshal errors\"):\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeStructure\n\tcase strings.Contains(lower, \"cannot unmarshal\"):\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeStructure\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeUnknown\n\t}\n}\n\n// checkStructuralIssues checks for potential structural issues in the YAML.\nfunc (v *Validator) checkStructuralIssues(node *yaml.Node, content string) []ValidationError {\n\tvar warnings []ValidationError\n\n\t// Recursively check for structural issues\n\tv.checkNode(node, content, &warnings, make(map[string]bool))\n\n\treturn warnings\n}\n\n// checkNode recursively checks a YAML node for structural issues.\nfunc (v *Validator) checkNode(node *yaml.Node, content string, warnings *[]ValidationError, seenKeys map[string]bool) {\n\tif node == nil {\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Check for duplicate keys in mappings\n\tif node.Kind == yaml.MappingNode {\n\t\tkeys := make(map[string]bool)\n\t\tfor i := 0; i < len(node.Content); i += 2 {\n\t\t\tif i+1 < len(node.Content) {\n\t\t\t\tkeyNode := node.Content[i]\n\t\t\t\tif keyNode.Kind == yaml.ScalarNode && keyNode.Value != \"\" {\n\t\t\t\t\tkey := keyNode.Value\n\t\t\t\t\tif keys[key] {\n\t\t\t\t\t\twarn := LocalValidationError{\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tFilePath: \"\", // Will be set by caller if needed\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tMessage:  fmt.Sprintf(\"Duplicate key detected: %q\", key),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tLine:     keyNode.Line,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tType:     ErrorTypeStructure,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tColumn:   keyNode.Column,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tContext:  fmt.Sprintf(\"Key %q appears multiple times in mapping\", key),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t\t*warnings = append(*warnings, warn.ToValidationError())\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\tkeys[key] = true\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Recursively check child nodes\n\tfor _, child := range node.Content {\n\t\tv.checkNode(child, content, warnings, seenKeys)\n\t}\n}\n\n// ValidateMultipleFiles validates multiple YAML files.\nfunc (v *Validator) ValidateMultipleFiles(filePaths []string) []ValidationResult {\n\tresults := make([]ValidationResult, 0, len(filePaths))\n\tfor _, path := range filePaths {\n\t\tresult := v.ValidateFile(path)\n\t\tresults = append(results, result)\n\t}\n\treturn results\n}\n","numLines":319,"startLine":1,"totalLines":319}}}

The type parameter T represents the type of the parsed data.\n44\t//\n45\t// This type is only used for successful parses - failed parses return error types.\n46\t// This design eliminates the need for Success/Error fields and provides type-safe\n47\t// access to the parsed data.\n48\t//\n49\t// Type parameter T is the type of the parsed data. Common choices include:\n50\t//   - map[string]interface{} for dynamic YAML structures\n51\t//   - []interface{} for YAML arrays\n52\t//   - A specific struct type for typed YAML parsing\n53\t//   - interface{} for fully generic content\n54\t//\n55\t// Example usage:\n56\t//   // Parse into a map\n57\t//   result := SuccessParseResult[map[string]interface{}]{...}\n58\t//   value := result.Data[\"key\"]\n59\t//\n60\t//   // Parse into a struct\n61\t//   type Config struct { Name string Port int }\n62\t//   result := SuccessParseResult[Config]{...}\n63\t//   fmt.Println(result.Data.Name)\n64\ttype SuccessParseResult[T any] struct {\n65\t\t// Raw is the original YAML content as bytes.\n66\t\t// Preserved for debugging, re-serialization, and validation purposes.\n67\t\t// May be nil if the parser chose not to retain the original content.\n68\t\tRaw []byte\n69\t\n70\t\t// Data is the successfully parsed YAML content.\n71\t\t// The type T is typically a struct type or map[string]interface{}.\n72\t\tData T\n73\t\n74\t\t// Source describes where the YAML content originated from.\n75\t\tSource ParseSource\n76\t\n77\t\t// Metadata contains information about the parsed YAML document structure.\n78\t\tMetadata ParseMetadata\n79\t\n80\t\t// Timing contains performance metrics for the parse operation.\n81\t\tTiming ParseTiming\n82\t}\n83\t\n84\t// ParseSource describes the origin of parsed YAML content.\n85\t//\n86\t// ParseSource provides information about where the YAML content came from,\n87\t// supporting files, strings, readers, and other sources.\n88\ttype ParseSource struct {\n89\t\t// Type indicates the kind of source (file, string, reader, etc.)\n90\t\tType SourceType\n91\t\n92\t\t// Path is the file path when Type is SourceFile, empty otherwise.\n93\t\tPath string\n94\t\n95\t\t// Description is a human-readable description of the source.\n96\t\t// For files, this is the filepath; for strings, it might be \"string\" or \"<stdin>\".\n97\t\tDescription string\n98\t\n99\t\t// Size is the size of the source content in bytes, if known.\n100\t\t// Zero indicates unknown size (e.g., from a reader).\n101\t\tSize int64\n102\t}\n103\t\n104\t// SourceType represents the type of YAML source.\n105\ttype SourceType string\n106\t\n107\tconst (\n108\t\t// SourceFile indicates YAML content from a file.\n109\t\tSourceFile SourceType = \"file\"\n110\t\n111\t\t// SourceString indicates YAML content from a string.\n112\t\tSourceString SourceType = \"string\"\n113\t\n114\t\t// SourceReader indicates YAML content from an io.Reader.\n115\t\tSourceReader SourceType = \"reader\"\n116\t\n117\t\t// SourceBytes indicates YAML content from a byte slice.\n118\t\tSourceBytes SourceType = \"bytes\"\n119\t\n120\t\t// SourceUnknown indicates an unknown or unspecified source type.\n121\t\tSourceUnknown SourceType = \"unknown\"\n122\t)\n123\t\n124\t// ParseMetadata contains structural information about parsed YAML content.\n125\t//\n126\t// ParseMetadata provides statistics and structural details about the YAML\n127\t// document that was parsed, useful for validation, debugging, and analytics.\n128\ttype ParseMetadata struct {\n129\t\t// LineCount is the total number of lines in the YAML source.\n130\t\tLineCount int\n131\t\n132\t\t// DocumentCount is the number of YAML documents in the source.\n133\t\t// Multi-document YAML files use \"---\" separators.\n134\t\tDocumentCount int\n135\t\n136\t\t// MaxNestingDepth is the maximum depth of nested structures found.\n137\t\t// A scalar at root level has depth 0; a field in a nested map has depth 1, etc.\n138\t\tMaxNestingDepth int\n139\t\n140\t\t// FieldCount is the total number of mapping fields across all documents.\n141\t\tFieldCount int\n142\t\n143\t\t// HasDocumentStart indicates whether the YAML begins with \"---\" marker.\n144\t\tHasDocumentStart bool\n145\t\n146\t\t// HasDocumentEnd indicates whether the YAML ends with \"...\" marker.\n147\t\tHasDocumentEnd bool\n148\t\n149\t\t// Encoding is the detected character encoding of the source.\n150\t\t// Typically \"utf-8\" for modern YAML files.\n151\t\tEncoding string\n152\t\n153\t\t// ContainsAnchors indicates whether the YAML contains YAML anchors/aliases (&/*/[]).\n154\t\tContainsAnchors bool\n155\t\n156\t\t// ContainsTags indicates whether the YAML contains explicit type tags (e.g., !!str).\n157\t\tContainsTags bool\n158\t}\n159\t\n160\t// ParseTiming contains performance metrics for parsing operations.\n161\t//\n162\t// ParseTiming provides detailed timing information for parsing operations,\n163\t// useful for performance analysis and optimization.\n164\ttype ParseTiming struct {\n165\t\t// Duration is the total time taken to parse the YAML content.\n166\t\tDuration time.Duration\n167\t\n168\t\t// ReadDuration is the time spent reading the source content.\n169\t\t// For files, this includes I/O time; for strings/bytes, this is typically zero.\n170\t\tReadDuration time.Duration\n171\t\n172\t\t// ParseDuration is the time spent actually parsing the YAML syntax.\n173\t\t// This excludes read time and validation time.\n174\t\tParseDuration time.Duration\n175\t\n176\t\t// ValidationDuration is the time spent validating the parsed structure.\n177\t\t// Zero if validation was not performed.\n178\t\tValidationDuration time.Duration\n179\t\n180\t\t// Timestamp is when the parse operation completed.\n181\t\tTimestamp time.Time\n182\t}\n183\t\n184\t// ============================================================================\n185\t// Methods on SuccessParseResult[T]\n186\t// ============================================================================\n187\t\n188\t// FilePath returns the source file path when the source is a file.\n189\t// Returns empty string for non-file sources.\n190\tfunc (spr SuccessParseResult[T]) FilePath() string {\n191\t\tif spr.Source.Type == SourceFile {\n192\t\t\treturn spr.Source.Path\n193\t\t}\n194\t\treturn \"\"\n195\t}\n196\t\n197\t// IsFile returns true if the parsed content originated from a file.\n198\tfunc (spr SuccessParseResult[T]) IsFile() bool {\n199\t\treturn spr.Source.Type == SourceFile\n200\t}\n201\t\n202\t// IsMultiDocument returns true if the YAML contains multiple documents.\n203\tfunc (spr SuccessParseResult[T]) IsMultiDocument() bool {\n204\t\treturn spr.Metadata.DocumentCount > 1\n205\t}\n206\t\n207\t// Size returns the size of the source content in bytes.\n208\t// Returns 0 if size is unknown.\n209\tfunc (spr SuccessParseResult[T]) Size() int64 {\n210\t\treturn spr.Source.Size\n211\t}\n212\t\n213\t// LineCount returns the number of lines in the YAML source.\n214\tfunc (spr SuccessParseResult[T]) LineCount() int {\n215\t\treturn spr.Metadata.LineCount\n216\t}\n217\t\n218\t// String returns a human-readable summary of the parse result.\n219\tfunc (spr SuccessParseResult[T]) String() string {\n220\t\tvar sb strings.Builder\n221\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"SuccessParseResult[%T]{\", spr.Data))\n222\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"Source: %s\", spr.Source.Description))\n223\t\tif spr.Source.Path != \"\" {\n224\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (path: %s)\", spr.Source.Path))\n225\t\t}\n226\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", Documents: %d\", spr.Metadata.DocumentCount))\n227\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", Lines: %d\", spr.Metadata.LineCount))\n228\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", Duration: %v\", spr.Timing.Duration))\n229\t\tsb.WriteString(\"}\")\n230\t\treturn sb.String()\n231\t}\n232\t\n233\t// ToLegacy converts the generic SuccessParseResult[T] to the legacy ParseResult format.\n234\t// This is provided for backward compatibility with code using the old ParseResult.\n235\tfunc (spr SuccessParseResult[T]) ToLegacy() ParseResult {\n236\t\treturn ParseResult{\n237\t\t\tFilePath:      spr.FilePath(),\n238\t\t\tData:          spr.Data,\n239\t\t\tSuccess:       true,\n240\t\t\tError:         nil,\n241\t\t\tParseDuration: spr.Timing.Duration,\n242\t\t\tMetrics: &ParseMetrics{\n243\t\t\t\tByteCount:       int(spr.Source.Size),\n244\t\t\t\tLineCount:       spr.Metadata.LineCount,\n245\t\t\t\tMaxNestingDepth: spr.Metadata.MaxNestingDepth,\n246\t\t\t\tKeyCount:        spr.Metadata.FieldCount,\n247\t\t\t},\n248\t\t}\n249\t}\n250\t\n251\t// GetRawBytes returns the raw YAML content as bytes.\n252\t// Returns nil if Raw is not set.\n253\tfunc (spr SuccessParseResult[T]) GetRawBytes() []byte {\n254\t\treturn spr.Raw\n255\t}\n256\t\n257\t// GetRawString returns the raw YAML content as a string.\n258\t// Returns empty string if Raw is nil.\n259\tfunc (spr SuccessParseResult[T]) GetRawString() string {\n260\t\tif spr.Raw == nil {\n261\t\t\treturn \"\"\n262\t\t}\n263\t\treturn string(spr.Raw)\n264\t}\n265\t\n266\t// HasRaw returns true if raw YAML content is available.\n267\tfunc (spr SuccessParseResult[T]) HasRaw() bool {\n268\t\treturn spr.Raw != nil\n269\t}\n270\t\n271\t// RawSize returns the size of the raw YAML content in bytes.\n272\t// Returns 0 if Raw is nil.\n273\tfunc (spr SuccessParseResult[T]) RawSize() int64 {\n274\t\tif spr.Raw == nil {\n275\t\t\treturn 0\n276\t\t}\n277\t\treturn int64(len(spr.Raw))\n278\t}\n279\t\n280\t// ============================================================================\n281\t// Methods on ParseSource\n282\t// ============================================================================\n283\t\n284\t// String returns a string representation of the source.\n285\tfunc (ps ParseSource) String() string {\n286\t\tif ps.Path != \"\" {\n287\t\t\treturn ps.Path\n288\t\t}\n289\t\treturn ps.Description\n290\t}\n291\t\n292\t// IsFile returns true if the source is a file.\n293\tfunc (ps ParseSource) IsFile() bool {\n294\t\treturn ps.Type == SourceFile\n295\t}\n296\t\n297\t// ============================================================================\n298\t// Methods on ParseMetadata\n299\t// ============================================================================\n300\t\n301\t// String returns a string representation of the metadata.\n302\tfunc (pm ParseMetadata) String() string {\n303\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"Metadata{Lines: %d, Docs: %d, Depth: %d, Fields: %d}\",\n304\t\t\tpm.LineCount, pm.DocumentCount, pm.MaxNestingDepth, pm.FieldCount)\n305\t}\n306\t\n307\t// ============================================================================\n308\t// Methods on ParseTiming\n309\t// ============================================================================\n310\t\n311\t// String returns a string representation of the timing metrics.\n312\tfunc (pt ParseTiming) String() string {\n313\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"Timing{Total: %v, Read: %v, Parse: %v, Validate: %v}\",\n314\t\t\tpt.Duration, pt.ReadDuration, pt.ParseDuration, pt.ValidationDuration)\n315\t}\n316\t\n317\t// IsZero returns true if the timing metrics are unset/zero.\n318\tfunc (pt ParseTiming) IsZero() bool {\n319\t\treturn pt.Duration == 0 && pt.ReadDuration == 0 && pt.ParseDuration == 0\n320\t}\n321\t\n322\t// ParseMetrics contains detailed metrics about parsing operations.\n323\ttype ParseMetrics struct {\n324\t\t// ByteCount is the size of the parsed file in bytes\n325\t\tByteCount int\n326\t\n327\t\t// LineCount is the number of lines in the YAML file\n328\t\tLineCount int\n329\t\n330\t\t// MaxNestingDepth is the maximum nesting depth found in the YAML\n331\t\tMaxNestingDepth int\n332\t\n333\t\t// bool\n338\t\n339\t\t// UnknownFields contains fields that were unknown during strict parsing\n340\t\tUnknownFields []string\n341\t}\n342\t\n343\t// IsFailure returns true if the parse operation failed.\n344\tfunc (pr ParseResult) IsFailure() bool {\n345\t\treturn !pr.Success\n346\t}\n347\t\n348\t// IsSuccess returns true if the parse operation succeeded.\n349\tfunc (pr ParseResult) IsSuccess() bool {\n350\t\treturn pr.Success\n351\t}\n352\t\n353\t// GetDetailedError returns detailed error information if available.\n354\t//\n355\t// This method attempts to extract detailed error information from the error,\n356\t// including line numbers, column information, and error context.\n357\tfunc (pr ParseResult) GetDetailedError() *DetailedParseError {\n358\t\tif pr.Error == nil {\n359\t\t\treturn nil\n360\t\t}\n361\t\n362\t\t// Try to extract detailed error from known error types\n363\t\tswitch err := pr.Error.(type) {\n364\t\tcase *ParseError:\n365\t\t\treturn &DetailedParseError{\n366\t\t\t\tFilePath:  err.FilePath,\n367\t\t\t\tLine:      err.Line,\n368\t\t\t\tColumn:    err.Column,\n369\t\t\t\tMessage:   err.Message,\n370\t\t\t\tErrorType: string(err.ErrorType),\n371\t\t\t}\n372\t\tcase *SyntaxError:\n373\t\t\treturn &DetailedParseError{\n374\t\t\t\tFilePath:  err.FilePath,\n375\t\t\t\tLine:      err.Line,\n376\t\t\t\tColumn:    err.Column,\n377\t\t\t\tMessage:   err.Message,\n378\t\t\t\tErrorType: \"syntax\",\n379\t\t\t\tExpected:  err.Expected,\n380\t\t\t\tFound:     err.Found,\n381\t\t\t}\n382\t\tcase *StructureError:\n383\t\t\treturn &DetailedParseError{\n384\t\t\t\tFilePath:  err.FilePath,\n385\t\t\t\tLine:      err.Line,\n386\t\t\t\tMessage:   err.Message,\n387\t\t\t\tErrorType: \"structure\",\n388\t\t\t\tContext:   err.Location,\n389\t\t\t}\n390\t\tcase *YAMLParseError:\n391\t\t\treturn &DetailedParseError{\n392\t\t\t\tFilePath:  err.FilePath,\n393\t\t\t\tLine:      err.Line,\n394\t\t\t\tColumn:    err.Column,\n395\t\t\t\tMessage:   err.Message,\n396\t\t\t\tErrorType: \"yaml_parse\",\n397\t\t\t}\n398\t\tdefault:\n399\t\t\treturn &DetailedParseError{\n400\t\t\t\tFilePath:  pr.FilePath,\n401\t\t\t\tMessage:   pr.Error.Error(),\n402\t\t\t\tErrorType: \"unknown\",\n403\t\t\t}\n404\t\t}\n405\t}\n406\t\n407\t// DetailedParseError provides detailed information about parsing errors.\n408\ttype DetailedParseError struct {\n409\t\tFilePath  string // Path to the file with the error\n410\t\tLine      int    // Line number where error occurred\n411\t\tColumn    int    // Column number where error occurred\n412\t\tMessage   string // Error message\n413\t\tErrorType string // Type of error that occurred\n414\t\tExpected  string // What was expected (for syntax errors)\n415\t\tFound     string // What was found (for syntax errors)\n416\t\tContext   string // Additional context about the error\n417\t}\n418\t\n419\t// String returns a formatted string representation of the detailed error.\n420\tfunc (dpe *DetailedParseError) String() string {\n421\t\tvar sb strings.Builder\n422\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"Parse error in %s\", dpe.FilePath))\n423\t\n424\t\tif dpe.Line > 0 {\n425\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" at line %d\", dpe.Line))\n426\t\t\tif dpe.Column > 0 {\n427\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", column %d\", dpe.Column))\n428\t\t\t}\n429\t\t}\n430\t\n431\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", dpe.Message))\n432\t\n433\t\tif dpe.Expected != \"\" || dpe.Found != \"\" {\n434\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (expected: %s, found: %s)\", dpe.Expected, dpe.Found))\n435\t\t}\n436\t\n437\t\tif dpe.Context != \"\" {\n438\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"\\nContext: %s\", dpe.Context))\n439\t\t}\n440\t\n441\t\treturn sb.String()\n442\t}\n443\t\n444\t// ValidationResult represents the result of validating a YAML file.\n445\t//\n446\t// ValidationResult provides comprehensive validation results including errors,\n447\t// warnings, and detailed validation metrics.\n448\ttype ValidationResult struct {\n449\t\t// FilePath is the path to the validated file\n450\t\tFilePath string\n451\t\n452\t\t// Valid indicates whether validation passed without errors\n453\t\tValid bool\n454\t\n455\t\t// Errors contains all validation errors found\n456\t\tErrors []ValidationError\n457\t\n458\t\t// Warnings contains all validation warnings found\n459\t\tWarnings []ValidationError\n460\t\n461\t\t// ValidationDuration is the time taken to validate (optional, for metrics)\n462\t\tValidationDuration time.Duration\n463\t\n464\t\t// SchemaVersion is the version of the schema used for validation (if applicable)\n465\t\tSchemaVersion string\n466\t\n467\t\t// ValidationMode is the mode used for validation\n468\t\tValidationMode string\n469\t}\n470\t\n471\t// HasErrors returns true if there are any validation errors.\n472\tfunc (vr ValidationResult) HasErrors() bool {\n473\t\treturn len(vr.Errors) > 0\n474\t}\n475\t\n476\t// HasWarnings returns true if there are any validation warnings.\n477\tfunc (vr ValidationResult) HasWarnings() bool {\n478\t\treturn len(vr.Warnings) > 0\n479\t}\n480\t\n481\t// ErrorCount returns the number of validation errors.\n482\tfunc (vr ValidationResult) ErrorCount() int {\n483\t\treturn len(vr.Errors)\n484\t}\n485\t\n486\t// WarningCount returns the number of validation warnings.\n487\tfunc (vr ValidationResult) WarningCount() int {\n488\t\treturn len(vr.Warnings)\n489\t}\n490\t\n491\t// ErrorSummary returns a formatted summary of all validation errors.\n492\tfunc (vr ValidationResult) ErrorSummary() string {\n493\t\tif !vr.HasErrors() {\n494\t\t\treturn \"No validation errors\"\n495\t\t}\n496\t\n497\t\tvar sb strings.Builder\n498\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"Validation failed for %s with %d error(s):\\n\", vr.FilePath, len(vr.Errors)))\n499\t\n500\t\tfor i, err := range vr.Errors {\n501\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%d. \", i+1))\n502\t\t\tsb.WriteString(err.String())\n503\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n504\t\t}\n505\t\n506\t\treturn sb.String()\n507\t}\n508\t\n509\t// WarningSummary returns a formatted summary of all validation warnings.\n510\tfunc (vr ValidationResult) WarningSummary() string {\n511\t\tif !vr.HasWarnings() {\n512\t\t\treturn \"No validation warnings\"\n513\t\t}\n514\t\n515\t\tvar sb strings.Builder\n516\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"Validation warnings for %s (%d warning(s)):\\n\", vr.FilePath, len(vr.Warnings)))\n517\t\n518\t\tfor i, warn := range vr.Warnings {\n519\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%d. \", i+1))\n520\t\t\tsb.WriteString(warn.String())\n521\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n522\t\t}\n523\t\n524\t\treturn sb.String()\n525\t}\n526\t\n527\t// FullSummary returns a complete summary including both errors and warnings.\n528\tfunc (vr ValidationResult) FullSummary() string {\n529\t\tvar sb strings.Builder\n530\t\n531\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"Validation result for %s:\\n\", vr.FilePath))\n532\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Valid: %v\\n\", vr.Valid))\n533\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Errors: %d\\n\", len(vr.Errors)))\n534\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Warnings: %d\\n\", len(vr.Warnings)))\n535\t\n536\t\tif vr.ValidationDuration > 0 {\n537\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Duration: %v\\n\", vr.ValidationDuration))\n538\t\t}\n539\t\n540\t\tif vr.SchemaVersion != \"\" {\n541\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Schema Version: %s\\n\", vr.SchemaVersion))\n542\t\t}\n543\t\n544\t\tif vr.HasErrors() {\n545\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n546\t\t\tsb.WriteString(vr.ErrorSummary())\n547\t\t}\n548\t\n549\t\tif vr.HasWarnings() {\n550\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n551\t\t\tsb.WriteString(vr.WarningSummary())\n552\t\t}\n553\t\n554\t\treturn sb.String()\n555\t}\n556\t\n557\t// SchemaValidationResult represents errors.\n609\tfunc (svr SchemaValidationResult) HasErrors() bool {\n610\t\treturn len(svr.Errors) > 0 || !svr.Valid\n611\t}\n612\t\n613\t// HasWarnings returns true if there are any validation warnings.\n614\tfunc (svr SchemaValidationResult) HasWarnings() bool {\n615\t\treturn len(svr.Warnings) > 0\n616\t}\n617\t\n618\t// ErrorSummary returns a formatted summary of all validation errors.\n619\tfunc (svr SchemaValidationResult) ErrorSummary() string {\n620\t\tif !svr.HasErrors() {\n621\t\t\treturn \"No schema validation errors\"\n622\t\t}\n623\t\n624\t\tvar sb strings.Builder\n625\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"Schema validation failed for %s:\\n\", svr.FilePath))\n626\t\n627\t\t// Missing required fields\n628\t\tif len(svr.MissingRequiredFields) > 0 {\n629\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"\\nMissing Required Fields (%d):\\n\", len(svr.MissingRequiredFields)))\n630\t\t\tfor i, field := range svr.MissingRequiredFields {\n631\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  %d. %s\\n\", i+1, field))\n632\t\t\t}\n633\t\t}\n634\t\n635\t\t// Type mismatches\n636\t\tif len(svr.TypeMismatches) > 0 {\n637\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"\\nType Mismatches (%d):\\n\", len(svr.TypeMismatches)))\n638\t\t\tfor i, tm := range svr.TypeMismatches {\n639\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  %d. %s: expected %s, got %s\\n\", i+1, tm.FieldPath, tm.ExpectedType, tm.ActualType))\n640\t\t\t}\n641\t\t}\n642\t\n643\t\t// Constraint violations\n644\t\tif len(svr.ConstraintViolations) > 0 {\n645\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"\\nConstraint Violations (%d):\\n\", len(svr.ConstraintViolations)))\n646\t\t\tfor i, cv := range svr.ConstraintViolations {\n647\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  %d. %s: %s\\n\", i+1, cv.FieldPath, cv.Message))\n648\t\t\t}\n649\t\t}\n650\t\n651\t\t// General errors\n652\t\tif len(svr.Errors) > 0 {\n653\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"\\nGeneral Errors (%d):\\n\", len(svr.Errors)))\n654\t\t\tfor i, err := range svr.Errors {\n655\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  %d. %s\\n\", i+1, err.Message))\n656\t\t\t}\n657\t\t}\n658\t\n659\t\treturn sb.String()\n660\t}\n661\t\n662\t// ProcessingResult represents the result of a complete YAML processing pipeline.\n663\t//\n664\t// {\n709\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"  Status: Success\\n\")\n710\t\t} else {\n711\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Status: Failed - %v\\n\", pr.ParseResult.Error))\n712\t\t}\n713\t\n714\t\tif {\n769\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"Error - %v\", far.Error))\n770\t\t} else if !far.Exists {\n771\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"Not found\")\n772\t\t} else if far.IsNil {\n773\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"Nil\")\n774\t\t} else {\n775\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%v (type: %s)\", far.Value, far.Type))\n776\t\t}\n777\t\n778\t\treturn sb.String()\n779\t}\n780\t\n781\t// files that passed validation.\n819\tfunc (bvr BatchValidationResult) SuccessRate() float64 {\n820\t\tif bvr.TotalFiles == 0 {\n821\t\t\treturn 0.0\n822\t\t}\n823\t\treturn (float64(bvr.ValidFiles) / float64(bvr.TotalFiles)) * 100.0\n824\t}\n825\t\n826\t// Summary returns a formatted summary of the batch validation results.\n827\tfunc (bvr BatchValidationResult) Summary() string {\n828\t\tvar sb bvr.InvalidFiles))\n834\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Total Errors: %d\\n\", bvr.TotalErrors))\n835\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Total Warnings: %d\\n\", bvr.TotalWarnings))\n836\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Success Rate: %.1f%%\\n\", bvr.SuccessRate()))\n837\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Total Duration: %v\\n\", bvr.TotalDuration))\n838\t\n839\t\tif bvr.HasErrors() {\n840\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\nFiles with errors:\\n\")\n841\t\t\tfor _, result := range bvr.Results {\n842\t\t\t\tif result.HasErrors() {\n843\t\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  - %s: %d error(s)\\n\", result.FilePath, result.ErrorCount()))\n844\t\t\t\t}\n845\t\t\t}\n846\t\t}\n847\t\n848\t\treturn sb.String()\n849\t}\n850\t\n851\t// GetFailedFiles returns a list of file paths that failed validation.\n852\tfunc (bvr BatchValidationResult) GetFailedFiles() []string {\n853\t\tvar failed []string\n854\t\tfor _, result := range bvr.Results {\n855\t\t\tif result.HasErrors() {\n856\t\t\t\tfailed = append(failed, result.FilePath)\n857\t\t\t}\n858\t\t}\n859\t\treturn result)\n869\t\t\t}\n870\t\t}\n871\t\treturn valid, invalid\n872\t}\n873\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"67aa1b08-ab21-45ef-a97d-2162813390b6","uuid":"bfa5fca6-116c-43a7-8ee2-353a5a6c52f5","timestamp":"2026-07-11T20:15:57.993Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/yamlutil/result_types.go","content":"// Package yamlutil provides comprehensive result type definitions for YAML processing operations.\n//\n// Result types provide structured return values for parsing, validation, and processing\n// operations, enabling detailed error reporting and result inspection.\npackage yamlutil\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"strings\"\n\t\"time\"\n)\n\n// ParseResult represents the result of parsing a YAML file.\n//\n// ParseResult provides a uniform return type for parsing operations that includes\n// success status, parsed data, and detailed error information.\n//\n// Deprecated: Use SuccessParseResult[T] for new code. This non-generic version is maintained\n// for backward compatibility.\ntype ParseResult struct {\n\t// FilePath is the path to the parsed file\n\tFilePath string\n\n\t// Data contains the parsed YAML data (usually map[string]interface{} or []interface{})\n\tData interface{}\n\n\t// Success indicates whether parsing completed successfully\n\tSuccess bool\n\n\t// Error contains any error that occurred during parsing\n\tError error\n\n\t// ParseDuration is the time taken to parse the file (optional, for metrics)\n\tParseDuration time.Duration\n\n\t// Metrics contains additional parsing metrics (optional)\n\tMetrics *ParseMetrics\n}\n\n// SuccessParseResult represents a successful YAML parsing outcome.\n//\n// SuccessParseResult[T] is a generic type that holds the result of successfully parsing\n// a YAML document. The type parameter T represents the type of the parsed data.\n//\n// This type is only used for successful parses - failed parses return error types.\n// This design eliminates the need for Success/Error fields and provides type-safe\n// access to the parsed data.\n//\n// Type parameter T is the type of the parsed data. Common choices include:\n//   - map[string]interface{} for dynamic YAML structures\n//   - []interface{} for YAML arrays\n//   - A specific struct type for typed YAML parsing\n//   - interface{} for fully generic content\n//\n// Example usage:\n//   // Parse into a map\n//   result := SuccessParseResult[map[string]interface{}]{...}\n//   value := result.Data[\"key\"]\n//\n//   // Parse into a struct\n//   type Config struct { Name string Port int }\n//   result := SuccessParseResult[Config]{...}\n//   fmt.Println(result.Data.Name)\ntype SuccessParseResult[T any] struct {\n\t// Raw is the original YAML content as bytes.\n\t// Preserved for debugging, re-serialization, and validation purposes.\n\t// May be nil if the parser chose not to retain the original content.\n\tRaw []byte\n\n\t// Data is the successfully parsed YAML content.\n\t// The type T is typically a struct type or map[string]interface{}.\n\tData T\n\n\t// Source describes where the YAML content originated from.\n\tSource ParseSource\n\n\t// Metadata contains information about the parsed YAML document structure.\n\tMetadata ParseMetadata\n\n\t// Timing contains performance metrics for the parse operation.\n\tTiming ParseTiming\n}\n\n// ParseSource describes the origin of parsed YAML content.\n//\n// ParseSource provides information about where the YAML content came from,\n// supporting files, strings, readers, and other sources.\ntype ParseSource struct {\n\t// Type indicates the kind of source (file, string, reader, etc.)\n\tType SourceType\n\n\t// Path is the file path when Type is SourceFile, empty otherwise.\n\tPath string\n\n\t// Description is a human-readable description of the source.\n\t// For files, this is the filepath; for strings, it might be \"string\" or \"<stdin>\".\n\tDescription string\n\n\t// Size is the size of the source content in bytes, if known.\n\t// Zero indicates unknown size (e.g., from a reader).\n\tSize int64\n}\n\n// SourceType represents the type of YAML source.\ntype SourceType string\n\nconst (\n\t// SourceFile indicates YAML content from a file.\n\tSourceFile SourceType = \"file\"\n\n\t// SourceString indicates YAML content from a string.\n\tSourceString SourceType = \"string\"\n\n\t// SourceReader indicates YAML content from an io.Reader.\n\tSourceReader SourceType = \"reader\"\n\n\t// SourceBytes indicates YAML content from a byte slice.\n\tSourceBytes SourceType = \"bytes\"\n\n\t// SourceUnknown indicates an unknown or unspecified source type.\n\tSourceUnknown SourceType = \"unknown\"\n)\n\n// ParseMetadata contains structural information about parsed YAML content.\n//\n// ParseMetadata provides statistics and structural details about the YAML\n// document that was parsed, useful for validation, debugging, and analytics.\ntype ParseMetadata struct {\n\t// LineCount is the total number of lines in the YAML source.\n\tLineCount int\n\n\t// DocumentCount is the number of YAML documents in the source.\n\t// Multi-document YAML files use \"---\" separators.\n\tDocumentCount int\n\n\t// MaxNestingDepth is the maximum depth of nested structures found.\n\t// A scalar at root level has depth 0; a field in a nested map has depth 1, etc.\n\tMaxNestingDepth int\n\n\t// FieldCount is the total number of mapping fields across all documents.\n\tFieldCount int\n\n\t// HasDocumentStart indicates whether the YAML begins with \"---\" marker.\n\tHasDocumentStart bool\n\n\t// HasDocumentEnd indicates whether the YAML ends with \"...\" marker.\n\tHasDocumentEnd bool\n\n\t// Encoding is the detected character encoding of the source.\n\t// Typically \"utf-8\" for modern YAML files.\n\tEncoding string\n\n\t// ContainsAnchors indicates whether the YAML contains YAML anchors/aliases (&/*/[]).\n\tContainsAnchors bool\n\n\t// ContainsTags indicates whether the YAML contains explicit type tags (e.g., !!str).\n\tContainsTags bool\n}\n\n// ParseTiming contains performance metrics for parsing operations.\n//\n// ParseTiming provides detailed timing information for parsing operations,\n// useful for performance analysis and optimization.\ntype ParseTiming struct {\n\t// Duration is the total time taken to parse the YAML content.\n\tDuration time.Duration\n\n\t// ReadDuration is the time spent reading the source content.\n\t// For files, this includes I/O time; for strings/bytes, this is typically zero.\n\tReadDuration time.Duration\n\n\t// ParseDuration is the time spent actually parsing the YAML syntax.\n\t// This excludes read time and validation time.\n\tParseDuration time.Duration\n\n\t// ValidationDuration is the time spent validating the parsed structure.\n\t// Zero if validation was not performed.\n\tValidationDuration time.Duration\n\n\t// Timestamp is when the parse operation completed.\n\tTimestamp time.Time\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Methods on SuccessParseResult[T]\n// ============================================================================\n\n// FilePath returns the source file path when the source is a file.\n// Returns empty string for non-file sources.\nfunc (spr SuccessParseResult[T]) FilePath() string {\n\tif spr.Source.Type == SourceFile {\n\t\treturn spr.Source.Path\n\t}\n\treturn \"\"\n}\n\n// IsFile returns true if the parsed content originated from a file.\nfunc (spr SuccessParseResult[T]) IsFile() bool {\n\treturn spr.Source.Type == SourceFile\n}\n\n// IsMultiDocument returns true if the YAML contains multiple documents.\nfunc (spr SuccessParseResult[T]) IsMultiDocument() bool {\n\treturn spr.Metadata.DocumentCount > 1\n}\n\n// Size returns the size of the source content in bytes.\n// Returns 0 if size is unknown.\nfunc (spr SuccessParseResult[T]) Size() int64 {\n\treturn spr.Source.Size\n}\n\n// LineCount returns the number of lines in the YAML source.\nfunc (spr SuccessParseResult[T]) LineCount() int {\n\treturn spr.Metadata.LineCount\n}\n\n// String returns a human-readable summary of the parse result.\nfunc (spr SuccessParseResult[T]) String() string {\n\tvar sb strings.Builder\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"SuccessParseResult[%T]{\", spr.Data))\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"Source: %s\", spr.Source.Description))\n\tif spr.Source.Path != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (path: %s)\", spr.Source.Path))\n\t}\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", Documents: %d\", spr.Metadata.DocumentCount))\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", Lines: %d\", spr.Metadata.LineCount))\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", Duration: %v\", spr.Timing.Duration))\n\tsb.WriteString(\"}\")\n\treturn sb.String()\n}\n\n// ToLegacy converts the generic SuccessParseResult[T] to the legacy ParseResult format.\n// This is provided for backward compatibility with code using the old ParseResult.\nfunc (spr SuccessParseResult[T]) ToLegacy() ParseResult {\n\treturn ParseResult{\n\t\tFilePath:      spr.FilePath(),\n\t\tData:          spr.Data,\n\t\tSuccess:       true,\n\t\tError:         nil,\n\t\tParseDuration: spr.Timing.Duration,\n\t\tMetrics: &ParseMetrics{\n\t\t\tByteCount:       int(spr.Source.Size),\n\t\t\tLineCount:       spr.Metadata.LineCount,\n\t\t\tMaxNestingDepth: spr.Metadata.MaxNestingDepth,\n\t\t\tKeyCount:        spr.Metadata.FieldCount,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n}\n\n// GetRawBytes returns the raw YAML content as bytes.\n// Returns nil if Raw is not set.\nfunc (spr SuccessParseResult[T]) GetRawBytes() []byte {\n\treturn spr.Raw\n}\n\n// GetRawString returns the raw YAML content as a string.\n// Returns empty string if Raw is nil.\nfunc (spr SuccessParseResult[T]) GetRawString() string {\n\tif spr.Raw == nil {\n\t\treturn \"\"\n\t}\n\treturn string(spr.Raw)\n}\n\n// HasRaw returns true if raw YAML content is available.\nfunc (spr SuccessParseResult[T]) HasRaw() bool {\n\treturn spr.Raw != nil\n}\n\n// RawSize returns the size of the raw YAML content in bytes.\n// Returns 0 if Raw is nil.\nfunc (spr SuccessParseResult[T]) RawSize() int64 {\n\tif spr.Raw == nil {\n\t\treturn 0\n\t}\n\treturn int64(len(spr.Raw))\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Methods on ParseSource\n// ============================================================================\n\n// String returns a string representation of the source.\nfunc (ps ParseSource) String() string {\n\tif ps.Path != \"\" {\n\t\treturn ps.Path\n\t}\n\treturn ps.Description\n}\n\n// IsFile returns true if the source is a file.\nfunc (ps ParseSource) IsFile() bool {\n\treturn ps.Type == SourceFile\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Methods on ParseMetadata\n// ============================================================================\n\n// String returns a string representation of the metadata.\nfunc (pm ParseMetadata) String() string {\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"Metadata{Lines: %d, Docs: %d, Depth: %d, Fields: %d}\",\n\t\tpm.LineCount, pm.DocumentCount, pm.MaxNestingDepth, pm.FieldCount)\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Methods on ParseTiming\n// ============================================================================\n\n// String returns a string representation of the timing metrics.\nfunc (pt ParseTiming) String() string {\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"Timing{Total: %v, Read: %v, Parse: %v, Validate: %v}\",\n\t\tpt.Duration, pt.ReadDuration, pt.ParseDuration, pt.ValidationDuration)\n}\n\n// IsZero returns true if the timing metrics are unset/zero.\nfunc (pt ParseTiming) IsZero() bool {\n\treturn pt.Duration == 0 && pt.ReadDuration == 0 && pt.ParseDuration == 0\n}\n\n// ParseMetrics contains detailed metrics about parsing operations.\ntype ParseMetrics struct {\n\t// ByteCount is the size of the parsed file in bytes\n\tByteCount int\n\n\t// LineCount is the number of lines in the YAML file\n\tLineCount int\n\n\t// MaxNestingDepth is the maximum nesting depth found in the YAML\n\tMaxNestingDepth int\n\n\t// KeyCount is the total number of keys in the YAML\n\tKeyCount int\n\n\t// HasDocumentStart indicates whether the YAML has a document start marker (---)\n\tHasDocumentStart bool\n\n\t// UnknownFields contains fields that were unknown during strict parsing\n\tUnknownFields []string\n}\n\n// IsFailure returns true if the parse operation failed.\nfunc (pr ParseResult) IsFailure() bool {\n\treturn !pr.Success\n}\n\n// IsSuccess returns true if the parse operation succeeded.\nfunc (pr ParseResult) IsSuccess() bool {\n\treturn pr.Success\n}\n\n// GetDetailedError returns detailed error information if available.\n//\n// This method attempts to extract detailed error information from the error,\n// including line numbers, column information, and error context.\nfunc (pr ParseResult) GetDetailedError() *DetailedParseError {\n\tif pr.Error == nil {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Try to extract detailed error from known error types\n\tswitch err := pr.Error.(type) {\n\tcase *ParseError:\n\t\treturn &DetailedParseError{\n\t\t\tFilePath:  err.FilePath,\n\t\t\tLine:      err.Line,\n\t\t\tColumn:    err.Column,\n\t\t\tMessage:   err.Message,\n\t\t\tErrorType: string(err.ErrorType),\n\t\t}\n\tcase *SyntaxError:\n\t\treturn &DetailedParseError{\n\t\t\tFilePath:  err.FilePath,\n\t\t\tLine:      err.Line,\n\t\t\tColumn:    err.Column,\n\t\t\tMessage:   err.Message,\n\t\t\tErrorType: \"syntax\",\n\t\t\tExpected:  err.Expected,\n\t\t\tFound:     err.Found,\n\t\t}\n\tcase *StructureError:\n\t\treturn &DetailedParseError{\n\t\t\tFilePath:  err.FilePath,\n\t\t\tLine:      err.Line,\n\t\t\tMessage:   err.Message,\n\t\t\tErrorType: \"structure\",\n\t\t\tContext:   err.Location,\n\t\t}\n\tcase *YAMLParseError:\n\t\treturn &DetailedParseError{\n\t\t\tFilePath:  err.FilePath,\n\t\t\tLine:      err.Line,\n\t\t\tColumn:    err.Column,\n\t\t\tMessage:   err.Message,\n\t\t\tErrorType: \"yaml_parse\",\n\t\t}\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn &DetailedParseError{\n\t\t\tFilePath:  pr.FilePath,\n\t\t\tMessage:   pr.Error.Error(),\n\t\t\tErrorType: \"unknown\",\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// DetailedParseError provides detailed information about parsing errors.\ntype DetailedParseError struct {\n\tFilePath  string // Path to the file with the error\n\tLine      int    // Line number where error occurred\n\tColumn    int    // Column number where error occurred\n\tMessage   string // Error message\n\tErrorType string // Type of error that occurred\n\tExpected  string // What was expected (for syntax errors)\n\tFound     string // What was found (for syntax errors)\n\tContext   string // Additional context about the error\n}\n\n// String returns a formatted string representation of the detailed error.\nfunc (dpe *DetailedParseError) String() string {\n\tvar sb strings.Builder\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"Parse error in %s\", dpe.FilePath))\n\n\tif dpe.Line > 0 {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" at line %d\", dpe.Line))\n\t\tif dpe.Column > 0 {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", column %d\", dpe.Column))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", dpe.Message))\n\n\tif dpe.Expected != \"\" || dpe.Found != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (expected: %s, found: %s)\", dpe.Expected, dpe.Found))\n\t}\n\n\tif dpe.Context != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"\\nContext: %s\", dpe.Context))\n\t}\n\n\treturn sb.String()\n}\n\n// ValidationResult represents the result of validating a YAML file.\n//\n// ValidationResult provides comprehensive validation results including errors,\n// warnings, and detailed validation metrics.\ntype ValidationResult struct {\n\t// FilePath is the path to the validated file\n\tFilePath string\n\n\t// Valid indicates whether validation passed without errors\n\tValid bool\n\n\t// Errors contains all validation errors found\n\tErrors []ValidationError\n\n\t// Warnings contains all validation warnings found\n\tWarnings []ValidationError\n\n\t// ValidationDuration is the time taken to validate (optional, for metrics)\n\tValidationDuration time.Duration\n\n\t// SchemaVersion is the version of the schema used for validation (if applicable)\n\tSchemaVersion string\n\n\t// ValidationMode is the mode used for validation\n\tValidationMode string\n}\n\n// HasErrors returns true if there are any validation errors.\nfunc (vr ValidationResult) HasErrors() bool {\n\treturn len(vr.Errors) > 0\n}\n\n// HasWarnings returns true if there are any validation warnings.\nfunc (vr ValidationResult) HasWarnings() bool {\n\treturn len(vr.Warnings) > 0\n}\n\n// ErrorCount returns the number of validation errors.\nfunc (vr ValidationResult) ErrorCount() int {\n\treturn len(vr.Errors)\n}\n\n// WarningCount returns the number of validation warnings.\nfunc (vr ValidationResult) WarningCount() int {\n\treturn len(vr.Warnings)\n}\n\n// ErrorSummary returns a formatted summary of all validation errors.\nfunc (vr ValidationResult) ErrorSummary() string {\n\tif !vr.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn \"No validation errors\"\n\t}\n\n\tvar sb strings.Builder\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"Validation failed for %s with %d error(s):\\n\", vr.FilePath, len(vr.Errors)))\n\n\tfor i, err := range vr.Errors {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%d. \", i+1))\n\t\tsb.WriteString(err.String())\n\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n\t}\n\n\treturn sb.String()\n}\n\n// WarningSummary returns a formatted summary of all validation warnings.\nfunc (vr ValidationResult) WarningSummary() string {\n\tif !vr.HasWarnings() {\n\t\treturn \"No validation warnings\"\n\t}\n\n\tvar sb strings.Builder\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"Validation warnings for %s (%d warning(s)):\\n\", vr.FilePath, len(vr.Warnings)))\n\n\tfor i, warn := range vr.Warnings {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%d. \", i+1))\n\t\tsb.WriteString(warn.String())\n\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n\t}\n\n\treturn sb.String()\n}\n\n// FullSummary returns a complete summary including both errors and warnings.\nfunc (vr ValidationResult) FullSummary() string {\n\tvar sb strings.Builder\n\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"Validation result for %s:\\n\", vr.FilePath))\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Valid: %v\\n\", vr.Valid))\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Errors: %d\\n\", len(vr.Errors)))\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Warnings: %d\\n\", len(vr.Warnings)))\n\n\tif vr.ValidationDuration > 0 {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Duration: %v\\n\", vr.ValidationDuration))\n\t}\n\n\tif vr.SchemaVersion != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Schema Version: %s\\n\", vr.SchemaVersion))\n\t}\n\n\tif vr.HasErrors() {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n\t\tsb.WriteString(vr.ErrorSummary())\n\t}\n\n\tif vr.HasWarnings() {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n\t\tsb.WriteString(vr.WarningSummary())\n\t}\n\n\treturn sb.String()\n}\n\n// SchemaValidationResult represents the result of schema-based validation.\n//\n// SchemaValidationResult extends ValidationResult with schema-specific\n// information including field type errors and constraint violations.\ntype SchemaValidationResult struct {\n\t// FilePath is the path to the validated file\n\tFilePath string\n\n\t// Valid indicates whether validation passed\n\tValid bool\n\n\t// Errors contains general validation errors\n\tErrors []SchemaValidationError\n\n\t// Warnings contains validation warnings\n\tWarnings []SchemaValidationError\n\n\t// MissingRequiredFields contains paths to required fields that are missing\n\tMissingRequiredFields []string\n\n\t// TypeMismatches contains type mismatch errors found during validation\n\tTypeMismatches []FieldTypeError\n\n\t// ConstraintViolations contains constraint violations found during validation\n\tConstraintViolations []ConstraintViolation\n\n\t// SchemaInfo contains information about the schema used for validation\n\tSchemaInfo *SchemaInfo\n}\n\n// SchemaInfo contains metadata about the schema used for validation.\ntype SchemaInfo struct {\n\t// SchemaName is the name of the schema\n\tSchemaName string\n\n\t// SchemaVersion is the version of the schema\n\tSchemaVersion string\n\n\t// SchemaPath is the path to the schema file (if loaded from file)\n\tSchemaPath string\n\n\t// ValidationMode is the validation mode used\n\tValidationMode SchemaMode\n\n\t// FieldsChecked is the number of fields validated\n\tFieldsChecked int\n\n\t// ConstraintsChecked is the number of constraints validated\n\tConstraintsChecked int\n}\n\n// HasErrors returns true if there are any validation errors.\nfunc (svr SchemaValidationResult) HasErrors() bool {\n\treturn len(svr.Errors) > 0 || !svr.Valid\n}\n\n// HasWarnings returns true if there are any validation warnings.\nfunc (svr SchemaValidationResult) HasWarnings() bool {\n\treturn len(svr.Warnings) > 0\n}\n\n// ErrorSummary returns a formatted summary of all validation errors.\nfunc (svr SchemaValidationResult) ErrorSummary() string {\n\tif !svr.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn \"No schema validation errors\"\n\t}\n\n\tvar sb strings.Builder\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"Schema validation failed for %s:\\n\", svr.FilePath))\n\n\t// Missing required fields\n\tif len(svr.MissingRequiredFields) > 0 {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"\\nMissing Required Fields (%d):\\n\", len(svr.MissingRequiredFields)))\n\t\tfor i, field := range svr.MissingRequiredFields {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  %d. %s\\n\", i+1, field))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Type mismatches\n\tif len(svr.TypeMismatches) > 0 {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"\\nType Mismatches (%d):\\n\", len(svr.TypeMismatches)))\n\t\tfor i, tm := range svr.TypeMismatches {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  %d. %s: expected %s, got %s\\n\", i+1, tm.FieldPath, tm.ExpectedType, tm.ActualType))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Constraint violations\n\tif len(svr.ConstraintViolations) > 0 {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"\\nConstraint Violations (%d):\\n\", len(svr.ConstraintViolations)))\n\t\tfor i, cv := range svr.ConstraintViolations {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  %d. %s: %s\\n\", i+1, cv.FieldPath, cv.Message))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// General errors\n\tif len(svr.Errors) > 0 {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"\\nGeneral Errors (%d):\\n\", len(svr.Errors)))\n\t\tfor i, err := range svr.Errors {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  %d. %s\\n\", i+1, err.Message))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn sb.String()\n}\n\n// ProcessingResult represents the result of a complete YAML processing pipeline.\n//\n// ProcessingResult combines results from parsing, validation, and processing\n// stages into a single comprehensive result.\ntype ProcessingResult struct {\n\t// FilePath is the path to the processed file\n\tFilePath string\n\n\t// Success indicates whether all processing stages completed successfully\n\tSuccess bool\n\n\t// ParseResult contains the parsing stage result\n\tParseResult ParseResult\n\n\t// ValidationResult contains the validation stage result (if validation was performed)\n\tValidationResult *ValidationResult\n\n\t// ProcessedData contains the final processed data\n\tProcessedData interface{}\n\n\t// TotalDuration is the total time taken for all processing stages\n\tTotalDuration time.Duration\n\n\t// StageResults contains results from individual processing stages\n\tStageResults map[string]interface{}\n}\n\n// HasParseErrors returns true if there were parse errors.\nfunc (pr ProcessingResult) HasParseErrors() bool {\n\treturn !pr.ParseResult.Success\n}\n\n// HasValidationErrors returns true if there were validation errors.\nfunc (pr ProcessingResult) HasValidationErrors() bool {\n\treturn pr.ValidationResult != nil && pr.ValidationResult.HasErrors()\n}\n\n// Summary returns a formatted summary of the processing result.\nfunc (pr ProcessingResult) Summary() string {\n\tvar sb strings.Builder\n\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"Processing result for %s:\\n\", pr.FilePath))\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Success: %v\\n\", pr.Success))\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Total Duration: %v\\n\", pr.TotalDuration))\n\n\tsb.WriteString(\"\\nParse Stage:\\n\")\n\tif pr.ParseResult.Success {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(\"  Status: Success\\n\")\n\t} else {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Status: Failed - %v\\n\", pr.ParseResult.Error))\n\t}\n\n\tif pr.ValidationResult != nil {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\nValidation Stage:\\n\")\n\t\tif pr.ValidationResult.Valid {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"  Status: Valid\\n\")\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"  Status: Invalid\\n\")\n\t\t}\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Errors: %d\\n\", pr.ValidationResult.ErrorCount()))\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Warnings: %d\\n\", pr.ValidationResult.WarningCount()))\n\t}\n\n\treturn sb.String()\n}\n\n// FieldAccessResult represents the result of accessing a field in YAML data.\n//\n// FieldAccessResult provides structured results for field access operations\n// including value, existence status, and error information.\ntype FieldAccessResult struct {\n\t// FieldPath is the dot-notation path to the field\n\tFieldPath string\n\n\t// Value is the retrieved field value\n\tValue interface{}\n\n\t// Exists indicates whether the field exists\n\tExists bool\n\n\t// Type is the type of the field value (if exists)\n\tType string\n\n\t// Error contains any error that occurred during field access\n\tError error\n\n\t// IsNil indicates whether the field value is nil\n\tIsNil bool\n}\n\n// IsSuccess returns true if field access succeeded.\nfunc (far FieldAccessResult) IsSuccess() bool {\n\treturn far.Error == nil && far.Exists && !far.IsNil\n}\n\n// IsMissing returns true if the field does not exist.\nfunc (far FieldAccessResult) IsMissing() bool {\n\treturn !far.Exists || far.IsNil\n}\n\n// String returns a formatted string representation of the field access result.\nfunc (far FieldAccessResult) String() string {\n\tvar sb strings.Builder\n\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"Field '%s': \", far.FieldPath))\n\n\tif far.Error != nil {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"Error - %v\", far.Error))\n\t} else if !far.Exists {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(\"Not found\")\n\t} else if far.IsNil {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(\"Nil\")\n\t} else {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%v (type: %s)\", far.Value, far.Type))\n\t}\n\n\treturn sb.String()\n}\n\n// BatchValidationResult represents the result of validating multiple YAML files.\n//\n// BatchValidationResult aggregates results from multiple file validations\n// and provides summary statistics.\ntype BatchValidationResult struct {\n\t// Results contains individual validation results for each file\n\tResults []ValidationResult\n\n\t// TotalFiles is the total number of files validated\n\tTotalFiles int\n\n\t// ValidFiles is the number of files that passed validation\n\tValidFiles int\n\n\t// InvalidFiles is the number of files that failed validation\n\tInvalidFiles int\n\n\t// TotalErrors is the total number of errors across all files\n\tTotalErrors int\n\n\t// TotalWarnings is the total number of warnings across all files\n\tTotalWarnings int\n\n\t// TotalDuration is the total time taken for all validations\n\tTotalDuration time.Duration\n}\n\n// HasErrors returns true if any file had validation errors.\nfunc (bvr BatchValidationResult) HasErrors() bool {\n\treturn bvr.TotalErrors > 0\n}\n\n// HasWarnings returns true if any file had validation warnings.\nfunc (bvr BatchValidationResult) HasWarnings() bool {\n\treturn bvr.TotalWarnings > 0\n}\n\n// SuccessRate returns the percentage of files that passed validation.\nfunc (bvr BatchValidationResult) SuccessRate() float64 {\n\tif bvr.TotalFiles == 0 {\n\t\treturn 0.0\n\t}\n\treturn (float64(bvr.ValidFiles) / float64(bvr.TotalFiles)) * 100.0\n}\n\n// Summary returns a formatted summary of the batch validation results.\nfunc (bvr BatchValidationResult) Summary() string {\n\tvar sb strings.Builder\n\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"Batch Validation Summary:\\n\"))\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Total Files: %d\\n\", bvr.TotalFiles))\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Valid Files: %d\\n\", bvr.ValidFiles))\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Invalid Files: %d\\n\", bvr.InvalidFiles))\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Total Errors: %d\\n\", bvr.TotalErrors))\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Total Warnings: %d\\n\", bvr.TotalWarnings))\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Success Rate: %.1f%%\\n\", bvr.SuccessRate()))\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Total Duration: %v\\n\", bvr.TotalDuration))\n\n\tif bvr.HasErrors() {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\nFiles with errors:\\n\")\n\t\tfor _, result := range bvr.Results {\n\t\t\tif result.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  - %s: %d error(s)\\n\", result.FilePath, result.ErrorCount()))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn sb.String()\n}\n\n// GetFailedFiles returns a list of file paths that failed validation.\nfunc (bvr BatchValidationResult) GetFailedFiles() []string {\n\tvar failed []string\n\tfor _, result := range bvr.Results {\n\t\tif result.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\tfailed = append(failed, result.FilePath)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn failed\n}\n\n// GetResultsByStatus returns results grouped by validation status.\nfunc (bvr BatchValidationResult) GetResultsByStatus() (valid, invalid []ValidationResult) {\n\tfor _, result := range bvr.Results {\n\t\tif result.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\tinvalid = append(invalid, result)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tvalid = append(valid, result)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn valid, invalid\n}\n","numLines":873,"startLine":1,"totalLines":873}}}

invalid YAML structure or formatting\"\n143\t\tcase ParseErrorVariantTypeMismatch:\n144\t\t\treturn \"Type mismatch error - cannot convert YAML to expected type\"\n145\t\tcase ParseErrorVariantValidation:\n146\t\t\treturn \"Validation error - constraint violation or invalid value\"\n147\t\tcase ParseErrorVariantIO:\n148\t\t\treturn \"I/O error - file operation failed\"\n149\t\tcase ParseErrorVariantStructure:\n150\t\t\treturn \"Structure error - invalid YAML document structure\"\n151\t\tcase ParseErrorVariantCustom:\n152\t\t\treturn \"Custom error - application-specific parsing error\"\n153\t\tdefault:\n154\t\t\treturn \"Unknown parse error variant\"\n155\t\t}\n156\t}\n157\t\n158\t// ErrorCode represents specific error codes for programmatic error handling.\n159\t//\n160\t// ErrorCodes provide machine-readable identifiers for errors, enabling\n161\t// programmatic error handling and internationalization of error messages.\n162\ttype ErrorCode string\n163\t\n164\tconst (\n165\t\t// ============================================================================\n166\t\t// File Error Codes\n167\t\t// ============================================================================\n168\t\n169\t\t// ErrCodeFileNotFound indicates that a file does not exist at the specified path.\n170\t\t// This error is returned when file operations fail because the target file cannot\n171\t\t// be found in the filesystem.\n172\t\tErrCodeFileNotFound ErrorCode = \"FILE_NOT_FOUND\"\n173\t\n174\t\t// ErrCodeFileAccessDenied indicates insufficient permissions to access a file.\n175\t\t// This error occurs when the application lacks the necessary read or write\n176\t\t// permissions for the specified file path.\n177\t\tErrCodeFileAccessDenied ErrorCode = \"FILE_ACCESS_DENIED\"\n178\t\n179\t\t// ErrCodeFileIOError indicates a generic file I/O error that doesn't fit\n180\t\t// other specific file error categories. This includes disk errors, filesystem\n181\t\t// issues, or other low-level I/O failures.\n182\t\tErrCodeFileIOError ErrorCode = \"FILE_IO_ERROR\"\n183\t\n184\t\t// ErrCodeFileEmpty indicates that a file exists but contains no data or\n185\t\t// only whitespace. This error is used when parsing an empty file would result\n186\t\t// in undefined behavior or missing required configuration.\n187\t\tErrCodeFileEmpty ErrorCode = \"FILE_EMPTY\"\n188\t\n189\t\t// ============================================================================\n190\t\t// Parse Error Codes\n191\t\t// ============================================================================\n192\t\n193\t\t// ErrCodeInvalidSyntax indicates a YAML syntax error during parsing.\n194\t\t// This includes invalid indentation, malformed YAML structure, invalid escape\n195\t\t// sequences, improper quoting, or any fundamental YAML syntax violation that\n196\t\t// prevents the document from being parsed.\n197\t\tErrCodeInvalidSyntax ErrorCode = \"INVALID_SYNTAX\"\n198\t\n199\t\t// ErrCodeTypeMismatch indicates a type conversion error during parsing.\n200\t\t// This occurs when YAML content cannot be converted to the expected Go type,\n201\t\t// such as attempting to parse a string as an integer, unmarshaling a scalar into\n202\t\t// a struct, or incompatible array/slice types.\n203\t\tErrCodeTypeMismatch ErrorCode = \"TYPE_MISMATCH\"\n204\t\n205\t\t// ErrCodeInvalidStructure indicates a YAML structure error that passes\n206\t\t// syntax checking but represents invalid document structure. This includes\n207\t\t// invalid nesting, inconsistent document structure, or other structural issues\n208\t\t// that prevent proper interpretation of the YAML data.\n209\t\tErrCodeInvalidStructure ErrorCode = \"INVALID_STRUCTURE\"\n210\t\n211\t\t// ErrCodeDuplicateKey indicates that a YAML mapping contains the same key\n212\t\t// multiple times. This is ambiguous and often indicates a configuration error,\n213\t\t// as YAML mappings should have unique keys.\n214\t\tErrCodeDuplicateKey ErrorCode = \"DUPLICATE_KEY\"\n215\t\n216\t\t// ErrCodeParseError indicates a generic parsing error that doesn't fit into\n217\t\t// more specific parse error categories. Use this as a fallback when the exact\n218\t\t// nature of the parse error cannot be determined.\n219\t\tErrCodeParseError ErrorCode = \"PARSE_ERROR\"\n220\t\n221\t\t// ============================================================================\n222\t\t// Validation Error Codes\n223\t\t// ============================================================================\n224\t\n225\t\t// ErrCodeValidationFailed indicates a general validation failure.\n226\t\t// This is used when validation cannot pass but doesn't fit into more specific\n227\t\t// validation error categories.\n228\t\tErrCodeValidationFailed ErrorCode = \"VALIDATION_FAILED\"\n229\t\n230\t\t// ErrCodeRequiredField indicates that a required field is missing from the\n231\t\t// YAML data. This error is used when accessing required fields that don't exist\n232\t\t// in the YAML structure, preventing proper configuration or processing.\n233\t\tErrCodeRequiredField ErrorCode = \"REQUIRED_FIELD\"\n234\t\n235\t\t// ErrCodeConstraintViolation indicates that a field value violates a defined\n236\t\t// constraint. This includes values out of allowed ranges, string length violations,\n237\t\t// pattern matching failures, or any constraint validation rule defined in the\n238\t\t// schema or validation logic.\n239\t\tErrCodeConstraintViolation ErrorCode = \"CONSTRAINT_VIOLATION\"\n240\t\n241\t\t// ErrCodeInvalidValue indicates that a field contains an invalid value that\n242\t\t// doesn't meet validation criteria. This is used when a value is syntactically\n243\t\t// correct but semantically invalid for the intended use.\n244\t\tErrCodeInvalidValue ErrorCode = \"INVALID_VALUE\"\n245\t\n246\t\t// ============================================================================\n247\t\t// Schema Error Codes\n248\t\t// ============================================================================\n249\t\n250\t\t// ErrCodeSchemaLoadFailed indicates an error occurred while loading a schema\n251\t\t// definition. This includes file I/O errors reading the schema file, JSON/YAML\n252\t\t// parsing errors in the schema definition itself, or invalid schema syntax.\n253\t\tErrCodeSchemaLoadFailed ErrorCode = \"SCHEMA_LOAD_FAILED\"\n254\t\n255\t\t// ErrCodeSchemaValidation indicates that YAML data failed validation against\n256\t\t// a schema. This error is returned when the YAML structure or content doesn't\n257\t\t// conform to the rules defined in the validation schema.\n258\t\tErrCodeSchemaValidation ErrorCode = \"SCHEMA_VALIDATION\"\n259\t\n260\t\t// ErrCodeSchemaNotFound indicates that a required schema file cannot be found.\n261\t\t// This error occurs when the schema reference points to a non-existent file or\n262\t\t// the schema cannot be located in the configured schema paths.\n263\t\tErrCodeSchemaNotFound ErrorCode = \"SCHEMA_NOT_FOUND\"\n264\t\n265\t\t// ErrCodeSchemaInvalid indicates that the schema definition itself is invalid.\n266\t\t// This includes schemas with invalid structure, unsupported features, or schemas\n267\t\t// that cannot be properly parsed or interpreted by the validation system.\n268\t\tErrCodeSchemaInvalid ErrorCode = \"SCHEMA_INVALID\"\n269\t)\n270\t\n271\t// ParseError represents errors that occur during YAML parsing.\n272\t//\n273\t// ParseError wraps underlying parsing errors and provides detailed\n274\t// location information (line, column) and context about what failed.\n275\ttype ParseError struct {\n276\t\tFilePath   string      // Path to the file being parsed\n277\t\tLine       int         // Line number where error occurred (1-indexed)\n278\t\tColumn     int         // Column number where error occurred (1-indexed)\n279\t\tMessage    string      // Human-readable error message\n280\t\tContextStr string      // Additional context about the parsing state\n281\t\tErr        error       // Underlying error for error wrapping\n282\t\tErrorType  ErrorType   // Specific type of parse error\n283\t\tErrorCode  ErrorCode   // Error code for programmatic handling\n284\t\tExpected   string      // What was expected (optional, for syntax/type errors)\n285\t\tActual     string      // What was actually found (optional, for syntax/type errors)\n286\t}\n287\t\n288\t// Code implements YAMLError interface.\n289\tfunc (pe *ParseError) Code() ErrorCode {\n290\t\tif pe.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n291\t\t\treturn pe.ErrorCode\n292\t\t}\n293\t\treturn ErrCodeParseError\n294\t}\n295\t\n296\t// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\n297\tfunc (pe *ParseError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n298\t\treturn pe.ErrorType\n299\t}\n300\t\n301\t// Context implements YAMLError interface.\n302\tfunc (pe *ParseError) Context() string {\n303\t\treturn pe.ContextStr\n304\t}\n305\t\n306\t// Error implements the error interface.\n307\tfunc (pe *ParseError) Error() string {\n308\t\tvar sb strings.Builder\n309\t\n310\t\t// Build base error with location\n311\t\tif pe.Line > 0 {\n312\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"parse error in %s at line %d\", pe.FilePath, pe.Line))\n313\t\t\tif pe.Column > 0 {\n314\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", column %d\", pe.Column))\n315\t\t\t}\n316\t\t} else {\n317\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"parse error in %s\", pe.FilePath))\n318\t\t}\n319\t\n320\t\t// Add message\n321\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", pe.Message))\n322\t\n323\t\t// Add expected vs actual if available\n324\t\tif pe.Expected != \"\" || pe.Actual != \"\" {\n325\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\" (\")\n326\t\t\tif pe.Expected != \"\" {\n327\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %s\", pe.Expected))\n328\t\t\t}\n329\t\t\tif pe.Expected != \"\" && pe.Actual != \"\" {\n330\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\", \")\n331\t\t\t}\n332\t\t\tif pe.Actual != \"\" {\n333\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"actual: %s\", pe.Actual))\n334\t\t\t}\n335\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\")\")\n336\t\t}\n337\t\n338\t\treturn sb.String()\n339\t}\n340\t\n341\t// Unwrap returns the underlying error for error wrapping chains.\n342\tfunc (pe *ParseError) Unwrap() error {\n343\t\treturn pe.Err\n344\t}\n345\t\n346\t// NewParseError creates a new ParseError with the given parameters.\n347\t//\n348\t// This constructor function provides a convenient way to create properly initialized\n349\t// ParseError instances with message, line, column, error code, and optional expected/actual values.\n350\t//\n351\t// Parameters:\n352\t//   - filePath: Path to the file being parsed\n353\t//   - message: Human-readable error message\n354\t//   - line: Line number where error occurred (1-indexed, use 0 if unknown)\n355\t//   - column: Column number where error occurred (1-indexed, use 0 if unknown)\n356\t//   - code: Error code for programmatic handling (use empty string for default)\n357\t//   - expected: What was expected (optional, use empty string if not applicable)\n358\t//   - actual: What was actually found (optional, use empty string if not applicable)\n359\t//\n360\t// Returns a properly initialized ParseError that implements the YAMLError interface.\n361\t//\n362\t// Example usage:\n363\t//\n364\t//\terr := NewParseError(\"config.yaml\", \"invalid syntax\", 10, 5, ErrCodeInvalidSyntax, \"identifier\", \"123\")\n365\tfunc NewParseError(filePath string, message string, line int, column int, code ErrorCode, expected string, actual string) *ParseError {\n366\t\t// Use provided code or default to generic parse error\n367\t\terrorCode := code\n368\t\tif errorCode == \"\" {\n369\t\t\terrorCode = ErrCodeParseError\n370\t\t}\n371\t\n372\t\treturn &ParseError{\n373\t\t\tFilePath:  filePath,\n374\t\t\tMessage:   message,\n375\t\t\tLine:      line,\n376\t\t\tColumn:    column,\n377\t\t\tErrorCode: errorCode,\n378\t\t\tErrorType: ErrorTypeParse,\n379\t\t\tExpected:  expected,\n380\t\t\tActual:    actual,\n381\t\t}\n382\t}\n383\t\n384\t// IsParseError checks if an error is a ParseError.\n385\tfunc IsParseError(err error) bool {\n386\t\tvar pe *ParseError\n387\t\treturn err != nil && (errors.As(err, &pe) || isYAMLErrorOfType(err, ErrorTypeParse))\n388\t}\n389\t\n390\t// ValidationError represents errors that occur during YAML validation.\n391\t//\n392\t// ValidationError wraps validation failures and provides detailed\n393\t// information about what failed validation. This is the base error type\n394\t// used throughout the yamlutil package for validation errors.\n395\ttype ValidationError struct {\n396\t\tFilePath   string    // Path to the file being validated\n397\t\tFieldPath  string    // Dot-notation path to the invalid field (optional)\n398\t\tMessage    string    // Human-readable error message\n399\t\tLine       int       // Line number where error occurred (1-indexed)\n400\t\tColumn     int       // Column number where error occurred (1-indexed, optional)\n401\t\tConstraint string    // Constraint that was violated (optional)\n402\t\tContextStr string    // Additional context about the validation state (optional)\n403\t\tErr        error     // Underlying error for error wrapping (optional)\n404\t\tErrorCode  ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling (optional)\n405\t\tType       ErrorType // Category of error for type switching\n406\t}\n407\t\n408\t// Code implements YAMLError interface.\n409\tfunc (ve *ValidationError) Code() ErrorCode {\n410\t\tif ve.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n411\t\t\treturn ve.ErrorCode\n412\t\t}\n413\t\treturn ErrCodeValidationFailed\n414\t}\n415\t\n416\t// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\n417\tfunc (ve *ValidationError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n418\t\t// If Type is explicitly set, use it\n419\t\tif ve.Type != \"\" {\n420\t\t\treturn ve.Type\n421\t\t}\n422\t\t// Try to determine error type from error code or message\n423\t\tif ve.ErrorCode == ErrCodeRequiredField || (ve.FieldPath != \"\" && containsRequiredFieldKeywords(ve.Message)) {\n424\t\t\treturn ErrorTypeFieldNotFound\n425\t\t}\n426\t\tif ve.ErrorCode == ErrCodeConstraintViolation || containsConstraintKeywords(ve.Message) {\n427\t\t\treturn ErrorTypeConstraint\n428\t\t}\n429\t\treturn ErrorTypeValidation\n430\t}\n431\t\n432\t// Context implements YAMLError interface.\n433\tfunc (ve *ValidationError) Context() string {\n434\t\treturn ve.ContextStr\n435\t}\n436\t\n437\t// Error implements the error interface.\n438\tfunc (ve *ValidationError) Error() string {\n439\t\tvar sb strings.Builder\n440\t\n441\t\t// Build base error with location\n442\t\tif ve.Line > 0 {\n443\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"validation error in %s at line %d\", ve.FilePath, ve.Line))\n444\t\t\tif ve.Column > 0 {\n445\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", column %d\", ve.Column))\n446\t\t\t}\n447\t\t} else {\n448\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"validation error in %s\", ve.FilePath))\n449\t\t}\n450\t\n451\t\t// Add field path if available\n452\t\tif ve.FieldPath != \"\" {\n453\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" at field %s\", ve.FieldPath))\n454\t\t}\n455\t\n456\t\t// Add message\n457\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", ve.Message))\n458\t\n459\t\t// Add constraint if available\n460\t\tif ve.Constraint != \"\" {\n461\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (constraint: %s)\", ve.Constraint))\n462\t\t}\n463\t\n464\t\treturn sb.String()\n465\t}\n466\t\n467\t// Unwrap returns the underlying error for error wrapping chains.\n468\tfunc (ve *ValidationError) Unwrap() error {\n469\t\treturn ve.Err\n470\t}\n471\t\n472\t// String returns a formatted error message with context.\n473\tfunc (ve *ValidationError) String() string {\n474\t\tvar sb strings.Builder\n475\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Error: %s\\n\", ve.Message))\n476\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Type: %s\\n\", ve.YAMLErrorType()))\n477\t\tif ve.Line > 0 {\n478\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Location: Line %d\", ve.Line))\n479\t\t\tif ve.Column > 0 {\n480\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", Column %d\", ve.Column))\n481\t\t\t}\n482\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n483\t\t}\n484\t\tif ve.FieldPath != \"\" {\n485\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Field: %s\\n\", ve.FieldPath))\n486\t\t}\n487\t\tif ve.Constraint != \"\" {\n488\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Constraint: %s\\n\", ve.Constraint))\n489\t\t}\n490\t\tif ve.ContextStr != \"\" {\n491\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Context: %s\\n\", ve.ContextStr))\n492\t\t}\n493\t\treturn sb.String()\n494\t}\n495\t\n496\t// NewValidationError creates a new ValidationError with the given parameters.\n497\t//\n498\t// This constructor function provides a convenient way to create properly initialized\n499\t// ValidationError instances with message, path, constraint, error code, and location.\n500\t//\n501\t// Parameters:\n502\t//   - filePath: Path to the file being validated\n503\t//   - message: Human-readable error message\n504\t//   - fieldPath: Dot-notation path to the invalid field (optional)\n505\t//   - constraint: Constraint that was violated (optional)\n506\t//   - code: Error code for programmatic handling (use empty string for default)\n507\t//   - line: Line number where error occurred (1-indexed, use 0 if unknown)\n508\t//   - column: Column number where error occurred (1-indexed, use 0 if unknown)\n509\t//   - errorType: Category of error (use empty string for default ErrorTypeValidation)\n510\t//\n511\t// Returns a properly initialized ValidationError that implements the YAMLError interface.\n512\t//\n513\t// Example usage:\n514\t//\n515\t//\terr := NewValidationError(\"config.yaml\", \"invalid port number\", \"server.port\", \"must be between 1-65535\", ErrCodeInvalidValue, 10, 5, \"\")\n516\tfunc NewValidationError(filePath string, message string, fieldPath string, constraint string, code ErrorCode, line int, column int, errorType ErrorType) *ValidationError {\n517\t\t// Use provided code or default to generic validation error\n518\t\terrorCode := code\n519\t\tif errorCode == \"\" {\n520\t\t\terrorCode = ErrCodeValidationFailed\n521\t\t}\n522\t\n523\t\t// Use provided error type or default to validation error\n524\t\teType := errorType\n525\t\tif eType == \"\" {\n526\t\t\teType = ErrorTypeValidation\n527\t\t}\n528\t\n529\t\treturn &ValidationError{\n530\t\t\tFilePath:   filePath,\n531\t\t\tMessage:    message,\n532\t\t\tFieldPath:  fieldPath,\n533\t\t\tConstraint: constraint,\n534\t\t\tErrorCode:  errorCode,\n535\t\t\tLine:       line,\n536\t\t\tColumn:     column,\n537\t\t\tType:       eType,\n538\t\t}\n539\t}\n540\t\n541\t// IsValidationError checks if an error is a ValidationError.\n542\tfunc IsValidationError(err error) bool {\n543\t\tvar ve *ValidationError\n544\t\treturn err != nil && (errors.As(err, &ve) || isYAMLErrorOfType(err, ErrorTypeValidation))\n545\t}\n546\t\n547\t// NewFileError creates a new FileError with proper initialization.\n548\tfunc NewFileError(path string, operation string, message string, errorCode ErrorCode) *FileError {\n549\t\treturn &FileError{\n550\t\t\tPath:       path,\n551\t\t\tOperation:  operation,\n552\t\t\tMessage:    message,\n553\t\t\tErrorCode:  errorCode,\n554\t\t}\n555\t}\n556\t\n557\t// NewSchemaValidationError creates a new SchemaValidationError with proper initialization.\n558\tfunc message,\n625\t\t\tLine:         line,\n626\t\t\tDuplicateKey: duplicateKey,\n627\t\t\tLocation:     location,\n628\t\t\tErrorCode:    errorCode,\n629\t\t}\n630\t}\n631\t\n632\t// NewDuplicateKeyError checks if the message suggests a required field error.\n691\tfunc containsRequiredFieldKeywords(msg string) bool {\n692\t\tkeywords := []string{\"required\", \"missing\", \"not found\", \"must be
