Package yamlutil provides comprehensive error type definitions for YAML processing.\n//\n// Error Hierarchy:\n//\n//\tYAMLError (base interface)\n//\t├── FileError (file I/O errors)\n//\t├── ParseError (YAML parsing errors)\n//\t│   ├── SyntaxError (YAML syntax errors)\n//\t│   ├── StructureError (YAML structure errors)\n//\t│   └── TypeMismatchError (type conversion errors)\n//\t├── ValidationError (validation errors)\n//\t│   ├── FieldNotFoundError (missing required fields)\n//\t│   ├── ConstraintError (constraint violations)\n//\t│   └── DuplicateKeyError (duplicate key errors)\n//\t└── SchemaError (schema-related errors)\n//\t\t├── SchemaLoadError (schema loading errors)\n//\t\t└── SchemaValidationError (schema validation errors)\npackage yamlutil\n\nimport (\n\t\"errors\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"os\"\n\t\"strings\"\n)\n\n// YAMLError is the base interface for all YAML processing errors.\n//\n// This interface provides a common type for all errors that occur during\n// YAML file I/O, parsing, validation, and schema operations.\ntype YAMLError interface {\n\terror\n\n\t// Code returns the error code for programmatic error handling.\n\tCode() ErrorCode\n\n\t// YAMLErrorType returns the category of error for type switching.\n\tYAMLErrorType() ErrorType\n\n\t// Context returns additional context about the error.\n\tContext() string\n}\n\n// ErrorType represents the category of YAML error.\ntype ErrorType string\n\nconst (\n\tErrorTypeFile           ErrorType = \"file\"           // File I/O errors\n\tErrorTypeParse          ErrorType = \"parse\"          // YAML parsing errors\n\tErrorTypeSyntax         ErrorType = \"syntax\"         // YAML syntax errors\n\tErrorTypeStructure      ErrorType = \"structure\"      // YAML structure errors\n\tErrorTypeTypeMismatch   ErrorType = \"type_mismatch\"  // Type conversion errors\n\tErrorTypeValidation     ErrorType = \"validation\"     // Validation errors\n\tErrorTypeFieldNotFound  ErrorType = \"field_not_found\" // Missing required fields\n\tErrorTypeConstraint     ErrorType = \"constraint\"     // Constraint violations\n\tErrorTypeDuplicateKey   ErrorType = \"duplicate_key\"  // Duplicate key errors\n\tErrorTypeSchema         ErrorType = \"schema\"         // Schema-related errors\n\tErrorTypeSchemaLoad     ErrorType = \"schema_load\"     // Schema loading errors\n\tErrorTypeSchemaValidate ErrorType = \"schema_validate\" // Schema validation errors\n\tErrorTypeUnknown        ErrorType = \"unknown\"        // Unknown error type\n\tErrorTypeEmpty          ErrorType = \"empty\"          // Empty content\n\tErrorTypeIO             ErrorType = \"io\"             // I/O error\n)\n\n// ParseErrorVariant represents the specific variant/category of a parse error.\n//\n// ParseErrorVariant provides a structured way to categorize different types of\n// parse errors, enabling precise error handling and user-friendly error messages.\n// Each variant represents a distinct category of parsing failure.\ntype ParseErrorVariant string\n\nconst (\n\t// ParseErrorVariantSyntax represents YAML syntax errors.\n\t//\n\t// This variant covers fundamental YAML syntax issues including:\n\t// - Invalid indentation\n\t// - Malformed YAML structure\n\t// - Invalid escape sequences\n\t// - Improper quoting\n\t// - Invalid YAML tokens\n\tParseErrorVariantSyntax ParseErrorVariant = \"syntax\"\n\n\t// ParseErrorVariantTypeMismatch represents type conversion errors.\n\t//\n\t// This variant covers errors when YAML content cannot be converted to the\n\t// expected Go type, including:\n\t// - Attempting to parse a string as an integer\n\t// - Trying to unmarshal a scalar into a struct\n\t// - Incompatible array/slice types\n\t// - Invalid boolean representations\n\tParseErrorVariantTypeMismatch ParseErrorVariant = \"type_mismatch\"\n\n\t// ParseErrorVariantValidation represents constraint validation errors.\n\t//\n\t// This variant covers errors when YAML data violates defined constraints,\n\t// including:\n\t// - Value out of allowed range\n\t// - String length violations\n\t// - Pattern matching failures\n\t// - Required field violations\n\tParseErrorVariantValidation ParseErrorVariant = \"validation\"\n\n\t// ParseErrorVariantIO represents I/O operation errors.\n\t//\n\t// This variant covers errors related to file operations during parsing,\n\t// including:\n\t// - File not found\n\t// - Permission denied\n\t// - Read/write failures\n\t// - File system errors\n\tParseErrorVariantIO ParseErrorVariant = \"io\"\n\n\t// ParseErrorVariantStructure represents YAML structure errors.\n\t//\n\t// This variant covers structural issues that syntax checking alone won't catch,\n\t// including:\n\t// - Duplicate mapping keys\n\t// - Invalid nesting\n\t// - Circular references\n\t// - Inconsistent document structure\n\tParseErrorVariantStructure ParseErrorVariant = \"structure\"\n\n\t// ParseErrorVariantCustom represents custom/extensible error categories.\n\t//\n\t// This variant is reserved for application-specific parsing errors that don't\n\t// fit into the standard categories. It enables extensibility for domain-specific\n\t// validation rules and custom parsing logic.\n\t// Applications can use this variant with custom error codes and messages\n\t// to represent specialized error conditions.\n\tParseErrorVariantCustom ParseErrorVariant = \"custom\"\n)\n\n// String returns the string representation of the ParseErrorVariant.\nfunc (v ParseErrorVariant) String() string {\n\treturn string(v)\n}\n\n// Description returns a human-readable description of the ParseErrorVariant.\nfunc (v ParseErrorVariant) Description() string {\n\tswitch v {\n\tcase ParseErrorVariantSyntax:\n\t\treturn \"YAML syntax error - invalid YAML structure or formatting\"\n\tcase ParseErrorVariantTypeMismatch:\n\t\treturn \"Type mismatch error - cannot convert YAML to expected type\"\n\tcase ParseErrorVariantValidation:\n\t\treturn \"Validation error - constraint violation or invalid value\"\n\tcase ParseErrorVariantIO:\n\t\treturn \"I/O error - file operation failed\"\n\tcase ParseErrorVariantStructure:\n\t\treturn \"Structure error - invalid YAML document structure\"\n\tcase ParseErrorVariantCustom:\n\t\treturn \"Custom error - application-specific parsing error\"\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn \"Unknown parse error variant\"\n\t}\n}\n\n// ErrorCode represents specific error codes for programmatic error handling.\n//\n// ErrorCodes provide machine-readable identifiers for errors, enabling\n// programmatic error handling and internationalization of error messages.\ntype ErrorCode string\n\nconst (\n\t// ============================================================================\n\t// File Error Codes\n\t// ============================================================================\n\n\t// ErrCodeFileNotFound indicates that a file does not exist at the specified path.\n\t// This error is returned when file operations fail because the target file cannot\n\t// be found in the filesystem.\n\tErrCodeFileNotFound ErrorCode = \"FILE_NOT_FOUND\"\n\n\t// ErrCodeFileAccessDenied indicates insufficient permissions to access a file.\n\t// This error occurs when the application lacks the necessary read or write\n\t// permissions for the specified file path.\n\tErrCodeFileAccessDenied ErrorCode = \"FILE_ACCESS_DENIED\"\n\n\t// ErrCodeFileIOError indicates a generic file I/O error that doesn't fit\n\t// other specific file error categories. This includes disk errors, filesystem\n\t// issues, or other low-level I/O failures.\n\tErrCodeFileIOError ErrorCode = \"FILE_IO_ERROR\"\n\n\t// ErrCodeFileEmpty indicates that a file exists but contains no data or\n\t// only whitespace. This error is used when parsing an empty file would result\n\t// in undefined behavior or missing required configuration.\n\tErrCodeFileEmpty ErrorCode = \"FILE_EMPTY\"\n\n\t// ============================================================================\n\t// Parse Error Codes\n\t// ============================================================================\n\n\t// ErrCodeInvalidSyntax indicates a YAML syntax error during parsing.\n\t// This includes invalid indentation, malformed YAML structure, invalid escape\n\t// sequences, improper quoting, or any fundamental YAML syntax violation that\n\t// prevents the document from being parsed.\n\tErrCodeInvalidSyntax ErrorCode = \"INVALID_SYNTAX\"\n\n\t// ErrCodeTypeMismatch indicates a type conversion error during parsing.\n\t// This occurs when YAML content cannot be converted to the expected Go type,\n\t// such as attempting to parse a string as an integer, unmarshaling a scalar into\n\t// a struct, or incompatible array/slice types.\n\tErrCodeTypeMismatch ErrorCode = \"TYPE_MISMATCH\"\n\n\t// ErrCodeInvalidStructure indicates a YAML structure error that passes\n\t// syntax checking but represents invalid document structure. This includes\n\t// invalid nesting, inconsistent document structure, or other structural issues\n\t// that prevent proper interpretation of the YAML data.\n\tErrCodeInvalidStructure ErrorCode = \"INVALID_STRUCTURE\"\n\n\t// ErrCodeDuplicateKey indicates that a YAML mapping contains the same key\n\t// multiple times. This is ambiguous and often indicates a configuration error,\n\t// as YAML mappings should have unique keys.\n\tErrCodeDuplicateKey ErrorCode = \"DUPLICATE_KEY\"\n\n\t// ErrCodeParseError indicates a generic parsing error that doesn't fit into\n\t// more specific parse error categories. Use this as a fallback when the exact\n\t// nature of the parse error cannot be determined.\n\tErrCodeParseError ErrorCode = \"PARSE_ERROR\"\n\n\t// ============================================================================\n\t// Validation Error Codes\n\t// ============================================================================\n\n\t// ErrCodeValidationFailed indicates a general validation failure.\n\t// This is used when validation cannot pass but doesn't fit into more specific\n\t// validation error categories.\n\tErrCodeValidationFailed ErrorCode = \"VALIDATION_FAILED\"\n\n\t// ErrCodeRequiredField indicates that a required field is missing from the\n\t// YAML data. This error is used when accessing required fields that don't exist\n\t// in the YAML structure, preventing proper configuration or processing.\n\tErrCodeRequiredField ErrorCode = \"REQUIRED_FIELD\"\n\n\t// ErrCodeConstraintViolation indicates that a field value violates a defined\n\t// constraint. This includes values out of allowed ranges, string length violations,\n\t// pattern matching failures, or any constraint validation rule defined in the\n\t// schema or validation logic.\n\tErrCodeConstraintViolation ErrorCode = \"CONSTRAINT_VIOLATION\"\n\n\t// ErrCodeInvalidValue indicates that a field contains an invalid value that\n\t// doesn't meet validation criteria. This is used when a value is syntactically\n\t// correct but semantically invalid for the intended use.\n\tErrCodeInvalidValue ErrorCode = \"INVALID_VALUE\"\n\n\t// ============================================================================\n\t// Schema Error Codes\n\t// ============================================================================\n\n\t// ErrCodeSchemaLoadFailed indicates an error occurred while loading a schema\n\t// definition. This includes file I/O errors reading the schema file, JSON/YAML\n\t// parsing errors in the schema definition itself, or invalid schema syntax.\n\tErrCodeSchemaLoadFailed ErrorCode = \"SCHEMA_LOAD_FAILED\"\n\n\t// ErrCodeSchemaValidation indicates that YAML data failed validation against\n\t// a schema. This error is returned when the YAML structure or content doesn't\n\t// conform to the rules defined in the validation schema.\n\tErrCodeSchemaValidation ErrorCode = \"SCHEMA_VALIDATION\"\n\n\t// ErrCodeSchemaNotFound indicates that a required schema file cannot be found.\n\t// This error occurs when the schema reference points to a non-existent file or\n\t// the schema cannot be located in the configured schema paths.\n\tErrCodeSchemaNotFound ErrorCode = \"SCHEMA_NOT_FOUND\"\n\n\t// ErrCodeSchemaInvalid indicates that the schema definition itself is invalid.\n\t// This includes schemas with invalid structure, unsupported features, or schemas\n\t// that cannot be properly parsed or interpreted by the validation system.\n\tErrCodeSchemaInvalid ErrorCode = \"SCHEMA_INVALID\"\n)\n\n// ParseError represents errors that occur during YAML parsing.\n//\n// ParseError wraps underlying parsing errors and provides detailed\n// location information (line, column) and context about what failed.\ntype ParseError struct {\n\tFilePath   string      // Path to the file being parsed\n\tLine       int         // Line number where error occurred (1-indexed)\n\tColumn     int         // Column number where error occurred (1-indexed)\n\tMessage    string      // Human-readable error message\n\tContextStr string      // Additional context about the parsing state\n\tErr        error       // Underlying error for error wrapping\n\tErrorType  ErrorType   // Specific type of parse error\n\tErrorCode  ErrorCode   // Error code for programmatic handling\n\tExpected   string      // What was expected (optional, for syntax/type errors)\n\tActual     string      // What was actually found (optional, for syntax/type errors)\n}\n\n// Code implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (pe *ParseError) Code() ErrorCode {\n\tif pe.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n\t\treturn pe.ErrorCode\n\t}\n\treturn ErrCodeParseError\n}\n\n// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (pe *ParseError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n\treturn pe.ErrorType\n}\n\n// Context implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (pe *ParseError) Context() string {\n\treturn pe.ContextStr\n}\n\n// Error implements the error interface.\nfunc (pe *ParseError) Error() string {\n\tvar sb strings.Builder\n\n\t// Build base error with location\n\tif pe.Line > 0 {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"parse error in %s at line %d\", pe.FilePath, pe.Line))\n\t\tif pe.Column > 0 {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", column %d\", pe.Column))\n\t\t}\n\t} else {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"parse error in %s\", pe.FilePath))\n\t}\n\n\t// Add message\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", pe.Message))\n\n\t// Add expected vs actual if available\n\tif pe.Expected != \"\" || pe.Actual != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(\" (\")\n\t\tif pe.Expected != \"\" {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %s\", pe.Expected))\n\t\t}\n\t\tif pe.Expected != \"\" && pe.Actual != \"\" {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\", \")\n\t\t}\n\t\tif pe.Actual != \"\" {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"actual: %s\", pe.Actual))\n\t\t}\n\t\tsb.WriteString(\")\")\n\t}\n\n\treturn sb.String()\n}\n\n// Unwrap returns the underlying error for error wrapping chains.\nfunc (pe *ParseError) Unwrap() error {\n\treturn pe.Err\n}\n\n// NewParseError creates a new ParseError with the given parameters.\n//\n// This constructor function provides a convenient way to create properly initialized\n// ParseError instances with message, line, column, error code, and optional expected/actual values.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - filePath: Path to the file being parsed\n//   - message: Human-readable error message\n//   - line: Line number where error occurred (1-indexed, use 0 if unknown)\n//   - column: Column number where error occurred (1-indexed, use 0 if unknown)\n//   - code: Error code for programmatic handling (use empty string for default)\n//   - expected: What was expected (optional, use empty string if not applicable)\n//   - actual: What was actually found (optional, use empty string if not applicable)\n//\n// Returns a properly initialized ParseError that implements the YAMLError interface.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terr := NewParseError(\"config.yaml\", \"invalid syntax\", 10, 5, ErrCodeInvalidSyntax, \"identifier\", \"123\")\nfunc NewParseError(filePath string, message string, line int, column int, code ErrorCode, expected string, actual string) *ParseError {\n\t// Use provided code or default to generic parse error\n\terrorCode := code\n\tif errorCode == \"\" {\n\t\terrorCode = ErrCodeParseError\n\t}\n\n\treturn &ParseError{\n\t\tFilePath:  filePath,\n\t\tMessage:   message,\n\t\tLine:      line,\n\t\tColumn:    column,\n\t\tErrorCode: errorCode,\n\t\tErrorType: ErrorTypeParse,\n\t\tExpected:  expected,\n\t\tActual:    actual,\n\t}\n}\n\n// IsParseError checks if an error is a ParseError.\nfunc IsParseError(err error) bool {\n\tvar pe *ParseError\n\treturn err != nil && (errors.As(err, &pe) || isYAMLErrorOfType(err, ErrorTypeParse))\n}\n\n// ValidationError represents errors that occur during YAML validation.\n//\n// ValidationError wraps validation failures and provides detailed\n// information about what failed validation. This is the base error type\n// used throughout the yamlutil package for validation errors.\ntype ValidationError struct {\n\tFilePath   string    // Path to the file being validated\n\tFieldPath  string    // Dot-notation path to the invalid field (optional)\n\tMessage    string    // Human-readable error message\n\tLine       int       // Line number where error occurred (1-indexed)\n\tColumn     int       // Column number where error occurred (1-indexed, optional)\n\tConstraint string    // Constraint that was violated (optional)\n\tContextStr string    // Additional context about the validation state (optional)\n\tErr        error     // Underlying error for error wrapping (optional)\n\tErrorCode  ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling (optional)\n\tType       ErrorType // Category of error for type switching\n}\n\n// Code implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (ve *ValidationError) Code() ErrorCode {\n\tif ve.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n\t\treturn ve.ErrorCode\n\t}\n\treturn ErrCodeValidationFailed\n}\n\n// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (ve *ValidationError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n\t// If Type is explicitly set, use it\n\tif ve.Type != \"\" {\n\t\treturn ve.Type\n\t}\n\t// Try to determine error type from error code or message\n\tif ve.ErrorCode == ErrCodeRequiredField || (ve.FieldPath != \"\" && containsRequiredFieldKeywords(ve.Message)) {\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeFieldNotFound\n\t}\n\tif ve.ErrorCode == ErrCodeConstraintViolation || containsConstraintKeywords(ve.Message) {\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeConstraint\n\t}\n\treturn ErrorTypeValidation\n}\n\n// Context implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (ve *ValidationError) Context() string {\n\treturn ve.ContextStr\n}\n\n// Error implements the error interface.\nfunc (ve *ValidationError) Error() string {\n\tvar sb strings.Builder\n\n\t// Build base error with location\n\tif ve.Line > 0 {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"validation error in %s at line %d\", ve.FilePath, ve.Line))\n\t\tif ve.Column > 0 {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", column %d\", ve.Column))\n\t\t}\n\t} else {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"validation error in %s\", ve.FilePath))\n\t}\n\n\t// Add field path if available\n\tif ve.FieldPath != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" at field %s\", ve.FieldPath))\n\t}\n\n\t// Add message\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", ve.Message))\n\n\t// Add constraint if available\n\tif ve.Constraint != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (constraint: %s)\", ve.Constraint))\n\t}\n\n\treturn sb.String()\n}\n\n// Unwrap returns the underlying error for error wrapping chains.\nfunc (ve *ValidationError) Unwrap() error {\n\treturn ve.Err\n}\n\n// String returns a formatted error message with context.\nfunc (ve *ValidationError) String() string {\n\tvar sb strings.Builder\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Error: %s\\n\", ve.Message))\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Type: %s\\n\", ve.YAMLErrorType()))\n\tif ve.Line > 0 {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Location: Line %d\", ve.Line))\n\t\tif ve.Column > 0 {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", Column %d\", ve.Column))\n\t\t}\n\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n\t}\n\tif ve.FieldPath != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Field: %s\\n\", ve.FieldPath))\n\t}\n\tif ve.Constraint != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Constraint: %s\\n\", ve.Constraint))\n\t}\n\tif ve.ContextStr != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Context: %s\\n\", ve.ContextStr))\n\t}\n\treturn sb.String()\n}\n\n// NewValidationError creates a new ValidationError with the given parameters.\n//\n// This constructor function provides a convenient way to create properly initialized\n// ValidationError instances with message, path, constraint, error code, and location.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - filePath: Path to the file being validated\n//   - message: Human-readable error message\n//   - fieldPath: Dot-notation path to the invalid field (optional)\n//   - constraint: Constraint that was violated (optional)\n//   - code: Error code for programmatic handling (use empty string for default)\n//   - line: Line number where error occurred (1-indexed, use 0 if unknown)\n//   - column: Column number where error occurred (1-indexed, use 0 if unknown)\n//   - errorType: Category of error (use empty string for default ErrorTypeValidation)\n//\n// Returns a properly initialized ValidationError that implements the YAMLError interface.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terr := NewValidationError(\"config.yaml\", \"invalid port number\", \"server.port\", \"must be between 1-65535\", ErrCodeInvalidValue, 10, 5, \"\")\nfunc NewValidationError(filePath string, message string, fieldPath string, constraint string, code ErrorCode, line int, column int, errorType ErrorType) *ValidationError {\n\t// Use provided code or default to generic validation error\n\terrorCode := code\n\tif errorCode == \"\" {\n\t\terrorCode = ErrCodeValidationFailed\n\t}\n\n\t// Use provided error type or default to validation error\n\teType := errorType\n\tif eType == \"\" {\n\t\teType = ErrorTypeValidation\n\t}\n\n\treturn &ValidationError{\n\t\tFilePath:   filePath,\n\t\tMessage:    message,\n\t\tFieldPath:  fieldPath,\n\t\tConstraint: constraint,\n\t\tErrorCode:  errorCode,\n\t\tLine:       line,\n\t\tColumn:     column,\n\t\tType:       eType,\n\t}\n}\n\n// IsValidationError checks if an error is a ValidationError.\nfunc IsValidationError(err error) bool {\n\tvar ve *ValidationError\n\treturn err != nil && (errors.As(err, &ve) || isYAMLErrorOfType(err, ErrorTypeValidation))\n}\n\n// NewFileError creates a new FileError with proper initialization.\nfunc NewFileError(path string, operation string, message string, errorCode ErrorCode) *FileError {\n\treturn &FileError{\n\t\tPath:       path,\n\t\tOperation:  operation,\n\t\tMessage:    message,\n\t\tErrorCode:  errorCode,\n\t}\n}\n\n// NewSchemaValidationError creates a new SchemaValidationError with proper initialization.\nfunc NewSchemaValidationError(filePath string, schemaPath string, fieldPath string, message string, expected string, found string, line int, errorCode ErrorCode) *SchemaValidationError {\n\treturn &SchemaValidationError{\n\t\tFilePath:   filePath,\n\t\tSchemaPath: schemaPath,\n\t\tFieldPath:  fieldPath,\n\t\tMessage:    message,\n\t\tExpected:   expected,\n\t\tFound:      found,\n\t\tLine:       line,\n\t\tErrorCode:  errorCode,\n\t}\n}\n\n// NewTypeMismatchError creates a new TypeMismatchError with proper initialization.\nfunc NewTypeMismatchError(filePath string, fieldPath string, expectedType string, actualType string, value string, line int, errorCode ErrorCode) *TypeMismatchError {\n\treturn &TypeMismatchError{\n\t\tFilePath:     filePath,\n\t\tFieldPath:    fieldPath,\n\t\tExpectedType: expectedType,\n\t\tActualType:   actualType,\n\t\tValue:        value,\n\t\tLine:         line,\n\t\tErrorCode:    errorCode,\n\t}\n}\n\n// NewFieldNotFoundError creates a new FieldNotFoundError with proper initialization.\nfunc NewFieldNotFoundError(filePath string, fieldPath string, line int, errorCode ErrorCode) *FieldNotFoundError {\n\treturn &FieldNotFoundError{\n\t\tFilePath:  filePath,\n\t\tFieldPath: fieldPath,\n\t\tLine:      line,\n\t\tErrorCode: errorCode,\n\t}\n}\n\n// NewConstraintError creates a new ConstraintError with proper initialization.\nfunc NewConstraintError(filePath string, fieldPath string, constraintType string, constraint string, value string, line int, errorCode ErrorCode) *ConstraintError {\n\treturn &ConstraintError{\n\t\tFilePath:       filePath,\n\t\tFieldPath:      fieldPath,\n\t\tConstraintType: constraintType,\n\t\tConstraint:     constraint,\n\t\tValue:          value,\n\t\tLine:           line,\n\t\tErrorCode:      errorCode,\n\t}\n}\n\n// NewSyntaxError creates a new SyntaxError with proper initialization.\nfunc NewSyntaxError(filePath string, message string, line int, column int, expected string, found string, errorCode ErrorCode) *SyntaxError {\n\treturn &SyntaxError{\n\t\tFilePath:  filePath,\n\t\tMessage:   message,\n\t\tLine:      line,\n\t\tColumn:    column,\n\t\tExpected:  expected,\n\t\tFound:     found,\n\t\tErrorCode: errorCode,\n\t}\n}\n\n// NewStructureError creates a new StructureError with proper initialization.\nfunc NewStructureError(filePath string, message string, line int, duplicateKey string, location string, errorCode ErrorCode) *StructureError {\n\treturn &StructureError{\n\t\tFilePath:     filePath,\n\t\tMessage:      message,\n\t\tLine:         line,\n\t\tDuplicateKey: duplicateKey,\n\t\tLocation:     location,\n\t\tErrorCode:    errorCode,\n\t}\n}\n\n// NewDuplicateKeyError creates a new DuplicateKeyError with proper initialization.\n//\n// This constructor function provides a convenient way to create properly initialized\n// DuplicateKeyError instances with the given parameters.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - filePath: Path to the file with duplicate keys\n//   - key: The duplicate key name\n//   - location: Nested path to the duplicate key (optional, use empty string if not applicable)\n//   - line1: Line number of first occurrence (use 0 if unknown)\n//   - line2: Line number of duplicate occurrence (use 0 if unknown)\n//   - code: Error code for programmatic handling (use empty string for default)\n//\n// Returns a properly initialized DuplicateKeyError that implements the YAMLError interface.\nfunc NewDuplicateKeyError(filePath string, key string, location string, line1 int, line2 int, code ErrorCode) *DuplicateKeyError {\n\t// Use provided code or default to duplicate key error\n\terrorCode := code\n\tif errorCode == \"\" {\n\t\terrorCode = ErrCodeDuplicateKey\n\t}\n\n\treturn &DuplicateKeyError{\n\t\tFilePath:  filePath,\n\t\tKey:       key,\n\t\tLocation:  location,\n\t\tLine1:     line1,\n\t\tLine2:     line2,\n\t\tErrorCode: errorCode,\n\t}\n}\n\n// NewSchemaLoadError creates a new SchemaLoadError with proper initialization.\n//\n// This constructor function provides a convenient way to create properly initialized\n// SchemaLoadError instances with the given parameters.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - filePath: Path to the schema file\n//   - message: Description of the load error\n//   - err: Underlying error (optional, use nil if not applicable)\n//   - code: Error code for programmatic handling (use empty string for default)\n//\n// Returns a properly initialized SchemaLoadError that implements the YAMLError interface.\nfunc NewSchemaLoadError(filePath string, message string, err error, code ErrorCode) *SchemaLoadError {\n\t// Use provided code or default to schema load failed error\n\terrorCode := code\n\tif errorCode == \"\" {\n\t\terrorCode = ErrCodeSchemaLoadFailed\n\t}\n\n\treturn &SchemaLoadError{\n\t\tFilePath:  filePath,\n\t\tMessage:   message,\n\t\tErr:       err,\n\t\tErrorCode: errorCode,\n\t}\n}\n\n// containsRequiredFieldKeywords checks if the message suggests a required field error.\nfunc containsRequiredFieldKeywords(msg string) bool {\n\tkeywords := []string{\"required\", \"missing\", \"not found\", \"must be provided\"}\n\tlower := strings.ToLower(msg)\n\tfor _, kw := range keywords {\n\t\tif strings.Contains(lower, kw) {\n\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn false\n}\n\n// containsConstraintKeywords checks if the message suggests a constraint violation.\nfunc containsConstraintKeywords(msg string) bool {\n\tkeywords := true\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn false\n}\n\n// FileError represents errors that occur during file I/O operations.\n//\n// FileError wraps file I/O errors and provides detailed\n// information about the file operation that failed.\ntype FileError struct {\n\tOp        string    // Operation that failed (read, write, etc.) - \"Op\" for backward compatibility\n\tPath      string    // Path to the file\n\tOperation string    // Operation that failed (read, write, etc.) - \"Operation\" for clarity\n\tMessage   string    // Human-readable error message\n\tErr       error     // Underlying error for error wrapping\n\tErrorCode ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling\n}\n\n// Code implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (fe *FileError) Code() ErrorCode {\n\tif fe.ErrorCode != \"\" fe.Path))\n\t}\n\n\tif fe.Message != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", fe.Message))\n\t}\n\n\tif fe.Err != nil {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", fe.Err.Error()))\n\t}\n\n\treturn sb.String()\n}\n\n// Unwrap returns the underlying error for error wrapping chains.\nfunc (fe *FileError) Unwrap() error {\n\treturn fe.Err\n}\n\n// IsFileError ============================================================================\n// EnhancedParseError Kind Checking Tests\n// ============================================================================\n\nfunc TestEnhancedParseErrorKindCheckers(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname      string\n\t\terr       *EnhancedParseError\n\t\tcheckFunc func(*EnhancedParseError) bool\n\t\twant      bool\n\t}{\n\t\t{\"syntax error is syntax\", NewSyntaxParseError(\"test.yaml\", \"err\", 1, 1, \"\", \"\"),\n\t\t\t(*EnhancedParseError).IsSyntaxError, true},\n\t\t{\"syntax error is not structure\", NewSyntaxParseError(\"test.yaml\", \"err\", 1, 1, \"\", \"\"),\n\t\t\t(*EnhancedParseError).IsStructureError, false},\n\t\t{\"structure error is structure\", NewStructureParseError(\"test.yaml\", \"err\", 1, \"\", \"\"),\n\t\t\t(*EnhancedParseError).IsStructureError, true},\n\t\t{\"structure error is not syntax\", NewStructureParseError(\"test.yaml\", \"err\", 1, \"\", \"\"),\n\t\t\t(*EnhancedParseError).IsSyntaxError, false},\n\t\t{\"type mismatch is type mismatch\", NewTypeMismatchParseError(\"test.yaml\", \"err\", 1, \"\", \"\", \"\", \"\"),\n\t\t\t(*EnhancedParseError).IsTypeMismatchError, true},\n\t\t{\"IO error is IO\", NewIOParseError(\"test.yaml\", \"err\", 0, nil),\n\t\t\t(*EnhancedParseError).IsIOError, true},\n\t\t{\"validation error is validation\", NewValidationParseError(\"test.yaml\", \"err\", 1, \"\", \"\", \"\"),\n\t\t\t(*EnhancedParseError).IsValidationError, true},\n\t\t{\"schema error is schema\", NewSchemaParseError(\"test.yaml\", \"err\", 1, \"\", \"\"),\n\t\t\t(*EnhancedParseError).IsSchemaError, true},\n\t\t{\"empty error is empty\", NewEmptyParseError(\"test.yaml\"),\n\t\t\t(*EnhancedParseError).IsEmpty, true},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\tif got := tt.checkFunc(tt.err); got != tt.want {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"check function returned %v, want %v\", got, tt.want)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// EnhancedParseError Legacy Conversion Tests\n// ============================================================================\n\nfunc TestEnhancedParseErrorToLegacyConversions(t *testing.T) {\n\tt.Run(\"to syntax error\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\tenhanced := NewSyntaxParseError(\"test.yaml\", \"bad syntax\", 5, 10, \":\", \"}\")\n\t\tlegacy := enhanced.ToLegacySyntaxError()\n\n\t\tif FieldPath='field', got '%s'\", legacy.FieldPath)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif legacy.ExpectedType != \"int\" {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected ExpectedType='int', got '%s'\", legacy.ExpectedType)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif ============================================================================\n// EnhancedParseError String Method Tests\n// ============================================================================\n\nfunc TestEnhancedParseErrorString(t *testing.T) {\n\tt.Run(\"error with snippet\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\terr := '%s'\", config.Key)\n\t}\n\n\t// Should panic with missing file\n\tdefer func() {\n\t\tif r := recover(); r == nil {\n\t\t\tt.Error(\"expected panic with missing file, got none\")\n\t\t}\n\t}()\n\n\tparser.MustParseFile(\"/nonexistent/file.yaml\", &config)\n}\n\nfunc TestNewStrictParser(t *testing.T) {\n\tparser := NewStrictParser()\n\n\tif parser == nil {\n\t\tt.Error(\"expected non-nil parser\")\n\t}\n\n\tif !parser.strict {\n\t\tt.Error(\"expected strict mode to be enabled\")\n\t}\n\n\ttmpDir := t.TempDir()\n\ttype TestConfig struct {\n\t\tName string `yaml:\"name\"`\n\t}\n\n\ttestYAML := `name: test`\n\ttestFile := filepath.Join(tmpDir, \"test.yaml\")\n\tif err := os.WriteFile(testFile, []byte(testYAML), 0644); err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to create test file: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tvar config TestConfig\n\tresult := parser.ParseFile(testFile, &config)\n\n\tif !result.Success {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected successful parse with strict parser, got error: %v\", result.Error)\n\t}\n\n\tif config.Name != \"test\" {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected name='test', got '%s'\", config.Name)\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestParseYAML(t *testing.T) {\n\ttmpDir := t.TempDir()\n\n\tt.Run(\"valid YAML file\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\ttestYAML := `\nkey1: value1\nkey2: value2\nnested:\n  item1: nested1\n  item2: nested2\n`\n\t\ttestFile := filepath.Join(tmpDir, \"test.yaml\")\n\t\tif err := os.WriteFile(testFile, []byte(testYAML), 0644); err != nil {\n\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to create test file: %v\", err)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tdata, err := ParseYAML(testFile)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"expected successful parse, got error: %v\", err)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif data[\"key1\"] != \"value1\" {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected key1='value1', got '%v'\", data[\"key1\"])\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tnested, ok := data[\"nested\"].(map[string]interface{})\n\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"expected nested to be map[string]interface{}, got %T\", data[\"nested\"])\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif nested[\"item1\"] != \"nested1\" {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected nested.item1='nested1', got '%v'\", nested[\"item1\"])\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\tt.Run(\"empty file returns empty map\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\ttestFile := filepath.Join(tmpDir, \"empty.yaml\")\n\t\tif err := os.WriteFile(testFile, []byte(\"\"), 0644); err != nil {\n\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to create test file: %v\", err)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tdata, err := ParseYAML(testFile)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"expected empty file to return no error, got: %v\", err)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif data == nil {\n\t\t\tt.Error(\"expected non-nil empty map\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif len(data) != 0 {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected empty map, got %d items\", len(data))\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\tt.Run(\"whitespace-only file returns empty map\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\ttestFile := filepath.Join(tmpDir, \"whitespace.yaml\")\n\t\tif err := os.WriteFile(testFile, []byte(\"   \\n\\t  \\n  \"), 0644); err != nil {\n\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to create test file: %v\", err)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tdata, err := ParseYAML(testFile)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"expected whitespace file to return no error, got: %v\", len(data))\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\tt.Run(\"file not found returns FileError\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t_, err := ParseYAML(\"/nonexistent/file.yaml\")\n\t\tif err == nil {\n\t\t\tt.Error(\"expected error for missing file, got nil\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Check if it's a FileError by checking the error message\n\t\terrMsg := err.Error()\n\t\tif !containsString(errMsg, \"read file\") && !containsString(errMsg, \"not found\") {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected file read error, got: %v\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\tt.Run(\"invalid YAML syntax returns YAMLParseError\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\tinvalidYAML := `key1: value1\nkey2: [unclosed bracket\nkey3: value3\n`\n\t\tinvalidFile := filepath.Join(tmpDir, \"invalid.yaml\")\n\t\tif err := os.WriteFile(invalidFile, []byte(invalidYAML), nil\")\n\t\t}\n\t})\n}\n\n// Helper function for string contains check\nfunc containsString(s, substr string) bool {\n\treturn len(s) >= len(substr) && (s == substr || len(s) > len(substr) && value2\n`\n\n\tresult := validator.ValidateString(yamlContent)\n\n\tif result.Valid {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Expected YAML with unexpected character to fail validation\")\n\t}\n\n\tif !result.HasErrors() {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Expected errors for unexpected character\")\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestValidator_EmptyContent(t *testing.T) {\n\tvalidator := NewValidator()\n\tyamlContent := \"\"\n\n\tresult := content\")\n\t}\n\n\terr := result.Errors[0]\n\tif err.Type != ErrorTypeEmpty {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected error type '%s', got: %s\", ErrorTypeEmpty, err.Type)\n\t}\n\n\tif TestValidator_ErrorFormatting(t *testing.T) {\n\tvalidator := NewValidator()\n\tyamlContent := `\nkey1: value1\n  invalid: indentation\nkey2: value2\n`\n\n\tresult := TestValidator_ValidateStringWithPath(t *testing.T) {\n\tvalidator := NewValidator()\n\tyamlContent := \"key: value\"\n\tfilePath := \"/test/path/config.yaml\"\n\n\tresult := err)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tvalidator := NewValidator()\n\tpaths := []string{\n\t\tfilepath.Join(tmpDir, \"valid1.yaml\"),\n\t\tfilepath.Join(tmpDir, \"valid2.yaml\"),\n\t\tfilepath.Join(tmpDir, \"invalid.yaml\"),\n\t}\n\n\tresults := validator.ValidateMultipleFiles(paths)\n\n\tif len(results) != len(paths) {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected %d results, got: %d\", len(paths), len(results))\n\t}\n\n\t// Verify first two files are valid\n\tfor i := 0; i < 2; i++ {\n\t\tif !results[i].Valid {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected file %d to be valid, got errors: %v\", i+1, results[i].Errors)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Verify third file is invalid\n\tif results[2].Valid {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Expected third file to be invalid\")\n\t}\n\n\tif !results[2].HasErrors() {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Expected third file to have errors\")\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestValidator_ErrorTypes(t *testing.T) {\n\ttestCases := []struct {\n\t\tname        string\n\t\tcontent true,\n\t\t\tWarnings: []ValidationError{\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tType:    ErrorTypeStructure,\n\t\t\t\t\tMessage: \"Duplicate key detected\",\n\t\t\t\t\tLine:    5,\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tType:    ErrorTypeValidation,\n\t\t\t\t\tMessage: \"Deprecated YAML feature\",\n\t\t\t\t\tLine:    10,\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tsummary := !strings.Contains(summary, \"2.\") {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected numbered warnings, got: %s\", summary)\n\t\t}\n\t})\n}\n\nfunc TestCategorizeError(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname         string\n\t\terrorMsg     string\n\t\texpectedType ErrorType\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:         \"could not find expected\",\n\t\t\terrorMsg:     \"yaml: line 1: could not find expected ':'\",\n\t\t\texpectedType: ErrorTypeSyntax,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:         \"did not find expected key\",\n\t\t\terrorMsg:     \"did not find expected key\",\n\t\t\texpectedType:    \"scanner error\",\n\t\t\terrorMsg:     \"scanner error\",\n\t\t\texpectedType: ErrorTypeSyntax,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:         \"unmarshal errors\",\n\t\t\terrorMsg:     \"unmarshal tt.errorMsg, result, tt.expectedType)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\n// Test acceptance criteria\nfunc TestAcceptanceCriteria_SyntaxValidationFunctional(t *testing.T) {\n\t// AC1: Syntax validation functional\n\tvalidator := NewValidator()\n\n\t// Valid YAML should pass\n\tvalidYAML := \"key: value\\nkey2: value2\"\n\tresult := validator.ValidateString(validYAML)\n\tif !result.Valid {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Valid YAML should pass validation\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Invalid YAML should fail\n\tinvalidYAML := \"key: value\\n  bad: indent\"\n\tresult = validator.ValidateString(invalidYAML)\n\tif result.Valid {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Invalid YAML should fail validation\")\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestAcceptanceCriteria_LineColumnContext(t *testing.T) {\n\t// AC2: Errors include line/column context\n\tvalidator := NewValidator()\n\tyamlContent := `key1: value1\nkey2: value2\nkey3: value3\n  bad_indentation: error\nkey4: value4`\n\n\tresult := validator.ValidateString(yamlContent)\n\n\tif !result.HasErrors() {\n\t\tt.Fatal(\"Expected errors for invalid YAML\")\n\t}\n\n\terr := result.Errors[0]\n\tif err.Line == 0 {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Expected line number to be reported\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Column may be 0 if parser doesn't provide it, but line should always be set\n}\n\nfunc TestAcceptanceCriteria_HumanReadableMessages(t *testing.T) {\n\t// AC3: Error messages are human-readable\n\tvalidator := NewValidator()\n\tyamlContent := `key1: value1\n  invalid_indent: error\nkey2: value2`\n\n\tresult := validator.ValidateString(yamlContent)\n\n\tif !result.HasErrors() {\n\t\tt.Fatal(\"Expected errors for invalid YAML\")\n\t}\n\n\terr := result.Errors[0]\n\n\t// Check message exists and is readable\n\tif err.Message == \"\" {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Expected non-empty error message\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Check formatted output includes clear sections\n\tformatted := err.String()\n\trequiredSections := []string{\"Error:\", \"Type:\", \"Location:\", \"Context:\"}\n\tfor _, section := range requiredSections {\n\t\tif !strings.Contains(formatted, section) {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected formatted error to contain '%s'\", section)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestAcceptanceCriteria_ErrorTypeCategorization(t *testing.T) {\n\t// AC4: Error types categorized\n\ttestCases := []struct {\n\t\tname          string\n\t\tcontent       string\n\t\texpectedTypes []ErrorType\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:          \"empty file\",\n\t\t\tcontent:       \"\",\n\t\t\texpectedTypes: []ErrorType{ErrorTypeEmpty},\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:          \"syntax error\",\n\t\t\tcontent:       \"key:\\n  subkey: value\\n  value_without_key\",\n\t\t\texpectedTypes: []ErrorType{ErrorTypeSyntax},\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tvalidator := NewValidator()\n\n\tfor _, tc := range testCases {\n\t\tt.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\tresult := validator.ValidateString(tc.content)\n\t\t\tif !result.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\t\tt.Fatal(\"Expected validation error\")\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\terrType := result.Errors[0].Type\n\t\t\tfound := false\n\t\t\tfor _, expected := range tc.expectedTypes {\n\t\t\t\tif errType == expected {\n\t\t\t\t\tfound = true\n\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif !found {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected error type to be one of %v, got: %s\", tc.expectedTypes, errType)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestWarningSummary_WithWarnings(t *testing.T) {\n\tvalidator := constraint (range, length, pattern, etc.)\n61\t\tConstraint     string // Description of constraint\n62\t\n63\t\t// Schema-specific fields\n64\t\tSchemaPath string // Path to schema file\n65\t\tSchemaName string // Name of schema\n66\t}\n67\t\n68\t// ErrorContext provides rich context about the error location.\n69\ttype ErrorContext struct {\n70\t\t// Line is the line number where error occurred (1-indexed)\n71\t\tLine int\n72\t\n73\t\t// Column is the column number where error occurred (1-indexed)\n74\t\tColumn int\n75\t\n76\t\t// Snippet is the line of source code where error occurred\n77\t\tSnippet string\n78\t\n79\t\t// SurroundingLines provides context around the error line\n80\t\tSurroundingLines []string\n81\t\n82\t\t// SnippetLineIndex is the index of the error line within SurroundingLines\n83\t\tSnippetLineIndex int\n84\t}\n85\t\n86\t// EnhancedParseError provides comprehensive error information for parsing failures.\n87\t//\n88\t// This enhanced error type includes:\n89\t// - Error kind categorization (syntax, structure, type mismatch, etc.)\n90\t// - Detailed error-specific information\n91\t// - Rich context including source snippets\n92\t// - Integration with Result<T, ParseError> pattern\n93\ttype EnhancedParseError struct {\n94\t\t// Kind categorizes the error type\n95\t\tKind ParseErrorKind\n96\t\n97\t\t// FilePath is the path to the file being parsed\n98\t\tFilePath string\n99\t\n100\t\t// Message is a human-readable error message\n101\t\tMessage string\n102\t\n103\t\t// Context provides location and source context information\n104\t\tLocationInfo ErrorContext\n105\t\n106\t\t// Detail contains error-specific details\n107\t\tDetail ParseErrorDetail\n108\t\n109\t\t// UnderlyingErr is the wrapped error for compatibility\n110\t\tUnderlyingErr error\n111\t}\n112\t\n113\t// Error implements the error interface.\n114\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) Error() string {\n115\t\tvar sb strings.Builder\n116\t\n117\t\t// Build error header\n118\t\tif e.LocationInfo.Line > 0 {\n119\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s error in %s at line %d\", e.Kind, e.FilePath, e.LocationInfo.Line))\n120\t\t\tif e.LocationInfo.Column > 0 {\n121\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", column %d\", e.LocationInfo.Column))\n122\t\t\t}\n123\t\t} else {\n124\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s error in %s\", e.Kind, e.FilePath))\n125\t\t}\n126\t\n127\t\t// Add kind-specific details\n128\t\tswitch e.Kind {\n129\t\tcase ParseErrorKindTypeMismatch:\n130\t\t\t// For type mismatch, if we have field info, format it specially\n131\t\t\tif e.Detail.FieldPath != \"\" {\n132\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", field %s: expected %s, got %s\",\n133\t\t\t\t\te.Detail.FieldPath, e.Detail.ExpectedType, e.Detail.ActualType))\n134\t\t\t\t// Add parenthetical with full details\n135\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (field: %s, expected: %s, got %s)\",\n136\t\t\t\t\te.Detail.FieldPath, e.Detail.ExpectedType, e.Detail.ActualType))\n137\t\t\t} else if e.Message != \"\" {\n138\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", e.Message))\n139\t\t\t}\n140\t\tcase ParseErrorKindSyntax:\n141\t\t\tif e.Message != \"\" {\n142\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", e.Message))\n143\t\t\t}\n144\t\t\tif e.Detail.Expected != \"\" || e.Detail.Found != \"\" {\n145\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (expected: %s, found: %s)\", e.Detail.Expected, e.Detail.Found))\n146\t\t\t}\n147\t\tcase ParseErrorKindStructure:\n148\t\t\tif e.Message != \"\" {\n149\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", e.Message))\n150\t\t\t}\n151\t\t\tif e.Detail.DuplicateKey != \"\" {\n152\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (duplicate key: %s\", e.Detail.DuplicateKey))\n153\t\t\t\tif e.Detail.Location != \"\" {\n154\t\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" at %s\", e.Detail.Location))\n155\t\t\t\t}\n156\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\")\")\n157\t\t\t}\n158\t\tcase ParseErrorKindValidation:\n159\t\t\tif e.Message != \"\" {\n160\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", e.Message))\n161\t\t\t}\n162\t\t\tif e.Detail.FieldPath != \"\" && e.Detail.Constraint != \"\" {\n163\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (field: %s, constraint: %s)\",\n164\t\t\t\t\te.Detail.FieldPath, e.Detail.Constraint))\n165\t\t\t}\n166\t\tdefault:\n167\t\t\t// For other error types, just add the message\n168\t\t\tif e.Message != \"\" {\n169\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", e.Message))\n170\t\t\t}\n171\t\t}\n172\t\n173\t\treturn sb.String()\n174\t}\n175\t\n176\t// Unwrap returns the underlying error.\n177\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) Unwrap() error {\n178\t\treturn e.UnderlyingErr\n179\t}\n180\t\n181\t// Code returns the error code for programmatic handling.\n182\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) Code() ErrorCode {\n183\t\tswitch e.Kind {\n184\t\tcase ParseErrorKindSyntax:\n185\t\t\treturn ErrCodeInvalidSyntax\n186\t\tcase ParseErrorKindStructure:\n187\t\t\tif e.Detail.DuplicateKey != \"\" {\n188\t\t\t\treturn ErrCodeDuplicateKey\n189\t\t\t}\n190\t\t\treturn ErrCodeInvalidStructure\n191\t\tcase ParseErrorKindTypeMismatch:\n192\t\t\treturn ErrCodeTypeMismatch\n193\t\tcase ParseErrorKindIO:\n194\t\t\treturn ErrCodeFileIOError\n195\t\tcase ParseErrorKindValidation:\n196\t\t\treturn ErrCodeValidationFailed\n197\t\tcase ParseErrorKindSchema:\n198\t\t\treturn ErrCodeSchemaValidation\n199\t\tcase ParseErrorKindEmpty:\n200\t\t\treturn ErrCodeFileEmpty\n201\t\tdefault:\n202\t\t\treturn ErrCodeParseError\n203\t\t}\n204\t}\n205\t\n206\t// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\n207\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n208\t\tswitch e.Kind {\n209\t\tcase ParseErrorKindSyntax:\n210\t\t\treturn ErrorTypeSyntax\n211\t\tcase ParseErrorKindStructure:\n212\t\t\treturn ErrorTypeStructure\n213\t\tcase ParseErrorKindTypeMismatch:\n214\t\t\treturn ErrorTypeTypeMismatch\n215\t\tcase ParseErrorKindIO:\n216\t\t\treturn ErrorTypeIO\n217\t\tcase ParseErrorKindValidation:\n218\t\t\treturn ErrorTypeValidation\n219\t\tcase ParseErrorKindSchema:\n220\t\t\treturn ErrorTypeSchema\n221\t\tcase ParseErrorKindEmpty:\n222\t\t\treturn ErrorTypeEmpty\n223\t\tdefault:\n224\t\t\treturn ErrorTypeUnknown\n225\t\t}\n226\t}\n227\t\n228\t// Context implements YAMLError interface.\n229\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) Context() string {\n230\t\tvar sb strings.Builder\n231\t\n232\t\tif e.LocationInfo.Line > 0 {\n233\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"line: %d\", e.LocationInfo.Line))\n234\t\t\tif e.LocationInfo.Column > 0 {\n235\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", column: %d\", e.LocationInfo.Column))\n236\t\t\t}\n237\t\t}\n238\t\n239\t\tif e.Detail.FieldPath != \"\" {\n240\t\t\tif sb.Len() > 0 {\n241\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\", \")\n242\t\t\t}\n243\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"field: %s\", e.Detail.FieldPath))\n244\t\t}\n245\t\n246\t\tif e.Detail.Location != \"\" {\n247\t\t\tif sb.Len() > 0 {\n248\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\", \")\n249\t\t\t}\n250\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"location: %s\", e.Detail.Location))\n251\t\t}\n252\t\n253\t\treturn sb.String()\n254\t}\n255\t\n256\t// String returns a detailed multi-line error representation with source snippet.\n257\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) String() string {\n258\t\tvar sb strings.Builder\n259\t\n260\t\t// Error header\n261\t\tsb.WriteString(e.Error())\n262\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n263\t\n264\t\t// Show snippet with caret pointer if we have it\n265\t\tif e.LocationInfo.Snippet != \"\" {\n266\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n267\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"  \")  // Add 2-space prefix for consistency\n268\t\t\tsb.WriteString(e.LocationInfo.Snippet)\n269\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n270\t\n271\t\t\t// Add column indicator if available\n272\t\t\tif e.LocationInfo.Column > 0 && e.LocationInfo.Column <= len(e.LocationInfo.Snippet) {\n273\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"  \")  // Add 2-space prefix for consistency\n274\t\t\t\tfor i := 0; i < e.LocationInfo.Column-1; i++ {\n275\t\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\" \")\n276\t\t\t\t}\n277\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"^--- here\\n\")\n278\t\t\t}\n279\t\t}\n280\t\n281\t\t// Add surrounding context if available\n282\t\tif len(e.LocationInfo.SurroundingLines) > 0 {\n283\t\t\t// Add blank line separator if we showed the snippet\n284\t\t\tif e.LocationInfo.Snippet != \"\" {\n285\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n286\t\t\t}\n287\t\t\tfor i, line := range e.LocationInfo.SurroundingLines {\n288\t\t\t\tif i == e.LocationInfo.SnippetLineIndex {\n289\t\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"> \")\n290\t\t\t\t} else {\n291\t\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"  \")\n292\t\t\t\t}\n293\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(line)\n294\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n295\t\t\t}\n296\t\t}\n297\t\n298\t\treturn sb.String()\n299\t}\n300\t\n301\t// IsSyntaxError checks if this is a syntax error.\n302\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsSyntaxError() bool {\n303\t\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindSyntax\n304\t}\n305\t\n306\t// IsStructureError checks if this is a structure error.\n307\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsStructureError() bool {\n308\t\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindStructure\n309\t}\n310\t\n311\t// IsTypeMismatchError checks if this is a type mismatch error.\n312\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsTypeMismatchError() bool {\n313\t\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindTypeMismatch\n314\t}\n315\t\n316\t// IsIOError checks if this is an I/O error.\n317\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsIOError() bool {\n318\t\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindIO\n319\t}\n320\t\n321\t// IsValidationError checks if this is a validation error.\n322\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsValidationError() bool {\n323\t\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindValidation\n324\t}\n325\t\n326\t// IsSchemaError checks if this is a schema error.\n327\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsSchemaError() bool {\n328\t\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindSchema\n329\t}\n330\t\n331\t// IsEmpty checks if this is an empty file error.\n332\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsEmpty() bool {\n333\t\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindEmpty\n334\t}\n335\t\n336\t// ============================================================================\n337\t// Error Construction Helpers\n338\t// ============================================================================\n339\t\n340\t// NewSyntaxParseError creates a new syntax error.\n341\tfunc NewSyntaxParseError(filePath, message string, line, column int, expected, found string) *EnhancedParseError {\n342\t\treturn &EnhancedParseError{\n343\t\t\tKind:     ParseErrorKindSyntax,\n344\t\t\tFilePath: filePath,\n345\t\t\tMessage:  message,\n346\t\t\tLocationInfo: ErrorContext{\n347\t\t\t\tLine:   line,\n348\t\t\t\tColumn: column,\n349\t\t\t},\n350\t\t\tDetail: ParseErrorDetail{\n351\t\t\t\tExpected: expected,\n352\t\t\t\tFound:    found,\n353\t\t\t},\n354\t\t}\n355\t}\n356\t\n357\t// NewStructureParseError creates a new structure error.\n358\tfunc NewStructureParseError(filePath, message string, line int, duplicateKey, message,\n363\t\t\tLocationInfo: ErrorContext{\n364\t\t\t\tLine: line,\n365\t\t\t},\n366\t\t\tDetail: ParseErrorDetail{\n367\t\t\t\tDuplicateKey: duplicateKey,\n368\t\t\t\tLocation: filePath,\n378\t\t\tMessage:  message,\n379\t\t\tLocationInfo: ErrorContext{\n380\t\t\t\tLine: line,\n381\t\t\t},\n382\t\t\tDetail: ParseErrorDetail{\n383\t\t\t\tFieldPath:    fieldPath,\n384\t\t\t\tExpectedType: expectedType,\n385\t\t\t\tActualType:   actualType,\n386\t\t\t\tValue:        value,\n387\t\t\t},\n388\t\t}\n389\t}\n390\t\n391\t// NewIOParseError creates a new ============================================================================\n447\t// Error Propagation Strategy\n448\t// ============================================================================\n449\t\n450\t// The error propagation strategy for ParseError follows these principles:\n451\t//\n452\t// 1. Always provide rich context: Include line numbers, column positions, and\n453\t//    source snippets whenever possible.\n454\t//\n455\t// 2. Preserve error chain: Use error wrapping (errors.Wrap, fmt.Errorf with %w)\n456\t//    to maintain the full error chain for debugging.\n457\t//\n458\t// 3. Type-safe error handling: Use Result[T, ParseError] pattern for compile-time\n459\t//    error handling guarantees.\n460\t//\n461\t// 4. Error transformation: Transform lower-level errors (e.g., yaml.v3 errors)\n462\t//    into EnhancedParseError with appropriate context and snippets.\n463\t//\n464\t// 5. Fail-fast on critical errors: I/O errors and syntax errors should immediately\n465\t//    return errors without attempting further processing.\n466\t//\n467\t// Error Flow:\n468\t//   Raw Error → EnhancedParseError (with context) → Result[T, ParseError]\n469\t//\n470\t// Example propagation:\n471\t//   yaml.Unmarshal error → extract line/column → read source snippet →\n472\t//   NewSyntaxParseError with snippet → Result[T, ParseError]\n473\t\n474\t// ============================================================================\n475\t// Integration with Legacy Error Types\n476\t// ============================================================================\n477\t\n478\t// ToLegacySyntaxError converts EnhancedParseError to legacy SyntaxError.\n479\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) ToLegacySyntaxError() *SyntaxError {\n480\t\tif e.Kind != ParseErrorKindSyntax {\n481\t\t\treturn nil\n482\t\t}\n483\t\treturn &SyntaxError{\n484\t\t\tFilePath:  e.FilePath,\n485\t\t\tLine:      e.LocationInfo.Line,\n486\t\t\tColumn:    e.LocationInfo.Column,\n487\t\t\tMessage:   e.Message,\n488\t\t\tExpected:  e.Detail.Expected,\n489\t\t\tFound:     e.Detail.Found,\n490\t\t\tErr:       e.UnderlyingErr,\n491\t\t\tErrorCode: e.Code(),\n492\t\t}\n493\t}\n494\t\n495\t// ToLegacyStructureError converts EnhancedParseError to legacy StructureError.\n496\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) ToLegacyStructureError() *StructureError {\n497\t\tif e.Kind != ParseErrorKindStructure {\n498\t\t\treturn nil\n499\t\t}\n500\t\treturn &StructureError{\n501\t\t\tFilePath:     e.FilePath,\n502\t\t\tLine:         e.LocationInfo.Line,\n503\t\t\tMessage:      e.Message,\n504\t\t\tDuplicateKey: e.Detail.DuplicateKey,\n505\t\t\tLocation:     e.Detail.Location,\n506\t\t\tErr:          e.UnderlyingErr,\n507\t\t\tErrorCode:    e.Code(),\n508\t\t}\n509\t}\n510\t\n511\t// ToLegacyTypeMismatchError converts EnhancedParseError to legacy TypeMismatchError.\n512\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) ToLegacyTypeMismatchError() *TypeMismatchError {\n513\t\tif e.Kind != ParseErrorKindTypeMismatch {\n514\t\t\treturn nil\n515\t\t}\n516\t\treturn &TypeMismatchError{\n517\t\t\tFilePath:     e.FilePath,\n518\t\t\tFieldPath:    e.Detail.FieldPath,\n519\t\t\tExpectedType: e.Detail.ExpectedType,\n520\t\t\tActualType:   e.Detail.ActualType,\n521\t\t\tValue:        e.Detail.Value,\n522\t\t\tLine:         e.LocationInfo.Line,\n523\t\t\tErrorCode:    e.Code(),\n524\t\t}\n525\t}\n526\t\n527\t// ToLegacyParseError converts EnhancedParseError to legacy ParseError.\n528\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) ToLegacyParseError() *ParseError {\n529\t\treturn &ParseError{\n530\t\t\tFilePath:   e.FilePath,\n531\t\t\tLine:       e.LocationInfo.Line,\n532\t\t\tColumn:     e.LocationInfo.Column,\n533\t\t\tMessage:    e.Message,\n534\t\t\tContextStr: e.Context(),\n535\t\t\tErr:        e.UnderlyingErr,\n536\t\t\tErrorType:  e.YAMLErrorType(),\n537\t\t\tErrorCode:  e.Code(),\n538\t\t}\n539\t}\n540\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"67aa1b08-ab21-45ef-a97d-2162813390b6","uuid":"201f18d4-8ba4-4e03-9086-6b101d25e4b5","timestamp":"2026-07-11T20:16:44.814Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/yamlutil/parse_error_design.go","content":"// Package yamlutil provides enhanced ParseError type design for failure cases.\n//\n// This file implements the comprehensive ParseError design that integrates\n// with the Result<T, ParseError> pattern for type-safe error handling.\npackage yamlutil\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"strings\"\n)\n\n// ParseErrorKind represents the category of parse error.\n// This serves as an enum-style discriminator for different error types.\ntype ParseErrorKind string\n\nconst (\n\t// ParseErrorKindSyntax indicates YAML syntax errors (indentation, malformed YAML, etc.)\n\tParseErrorKindSyntax ParseErrorKind = \"syntax\"\n\n\t// ParseErrorKindStructure indicates YAML structure errors (duplicate keys, invalid nesting, etc.)\n\tParseErrorKindStructure ParseErrorKind = \"structure\"\n\n\t// ParseErrorKindTypeMismatch indicates type conversion errors (string to int, etc.)\n\tParseErrorKindTypeMismatch ParseErrorKind = \"type mismatch\"\n\n\t// ParseErrorKindIO indicates file I/O errors (not found, permission denied, etc.)\n\tParseErrorKindIO ParseErrorKind = \"io\"\n\n\t// ParseErrorKindValidation indicates semantic validation errors (required fields, constraints, etc.)\n\tParseErrorKindValidation ParseErrorKind = \"validation\"\n\n\t// ParseErrorKindSchema indicates schema validation errors\n\tParseErrorKindSchema ParseErrorKind = \"schema\"\n\n\t// ParseErrorKindEmpty indicates empty file or content\n\tParseErrorKindEmpty ParseErrorKind = \"empty\"\n\n\t// ParseErrorKindUnknown indicates an unknown error type\n\tParseErrorKindUnknown ParseErrorKind = \"unknown\"\n)\n\n// ParseErrorDetail contains specific details for different error kinds.\n// Only relevant fields are populated based on the ErrorKind.\ntype ParseErrorDetail struct {\n\t// Syntax-specific fields\n\tExpected string // What was expected (for syntax errors)\n\tFound    string // What was actually found\n\n\t// Structure-specific fields\n\tDuplicateKey string // Name of duplicate key (for structure errors)\n\tLocation     string // Nested path to error location\n\n\t// Type mismatch-specific fields\n\tFieldPath    string // Dot-notation path to field (for type errors)\n\tExpectedType string // Expected type\n\tActualType   string // Actual type found\n\tValue        string // Actual value that caused error\n\n\t// Validation-specific fields\n\tConstraintType string // Type of constraint (range, length, pattern, etc.)\n\tConstraint     string // Description of constraint\n\n\t// Schema-specific fields\n\tSchemaPath string // Path to schema file\n\tSchemaName string // Name of schema\n}\n\n// ErrorContext provides rich context about the error location.\ntype ErrorContext struct {\n\t// Line is the line number where error occurred (1-indexed)\n\tLine int\n\n\t// Column is the column number where error occurred (1-indexed)\n\tColumn int\n\n\t// Snippet is the line of source code where error occurred\n\tSnippet string\n\n\t// SurroundingLines provides context around the error line\n\tSurroundingLines []string\n\n\t// SnippetLineIndex is the index of the error line within SurroundingLines\n\tSnippetLineIndex int\n}\n\n// EnhancedParseError provides comprehensive error information for parsing failures.\n//\n// This enhanced error type includes:\n// - Error kind categorization (syntax, structure, type mismatch, etc.)\n// - Detailed error-specific information\n// - Rich context including source snippets\n// - Integration with Result<T, ParseError> pattern\ntype EnhancedParseError struct {\n\t// Kind categorizes the error type\n\tKind ParseErrorKind\n\n\t// FilePath is the path to the file being parsed\n\tFilePath string\n\n\t// Message is a human-readable error message\n\tMessage string\n\n\t// Context provides location and source context information\n\tLocationInfo ErrorContext\n\n\t// Detail contains error-specific details\n\tDetail ParseErrorDetail\n\n\t// UnderlyingErr is the wrapped error for compatibility\n\tUnderlyingErr error\n}\n\n// Error implements the error interface.\nfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) Error() string {\n\tvar sb strings.Builder\n\n\t// Build error header\n\tif e.LocationInfo.Line > 0 {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s error in %s at line %d\", e.Kind, e.FilePath, e.LocationInfo.Line))\n\t\tif e.LocationInfo.Column > 0 {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", column %d\", e.LocationInfo.Column))\n\t\t}\n\t} else {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s error in %s\", e.Kind, e.FilePath))\n\t}\n\n\t// Add kind-specific details\n\tswitch e.Kind {\n\tcase ParseErrorKindTypeMismatch:\n\t\t// For type mismatch, if we have field info, format it specially\n\t\tif e.Detail.FieldPath != \"\" {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", field %s: expected %s, got %s\",\n\t\t\t\te.Detail.FieldPath, e.Detail.ExpectedType, e.Detail.ActualType))\n\t\t\t// Add parenthetical with full details\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (field: %s, expected: %s, got %s)\",\n\t\t\t\te.Detail.FieldPath, e.Detail.ExpectedType, e.Detail.ActualType))\n\t\t} else if e.Message != \"\" {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", e.Message))\n\t\t}\n\tcase ParseErrorKindSyntax:\n\t\tif e.Message != \"\" {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", e.Message))\n\t\t}\n\t\tif e.Detail.Expected != \"\" || e.Detail.Found != \"\" {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (expected: %s, found: %s)\", e.Detail.Expected, e.Detail.Found))\n\t\t}\n\tcase ParseErrorKindStructure:\n\t\tif e.Message != \"\" {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", e.Message))\n\t\t}\n\t\tif e.Detail.DuplicateKey != \"\" {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (duplicate key: %s\", e.Detail.DuplicateKey))\n\t\t\tif e.Detail.Location != \"\" {\n\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" at %s\", e.Detail.Location))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\")\")\n\t\t}\n\tcase ParseErrorKindValidation:\n\t\tif e.Message != \"\" {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", e.Message))\n\t\t}\n\t\tif e.Detail.FieldPath != \"\" && e.Detail.Constraint != \"\" {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (field: %s, constraint: %s)\",\n\t\t\t\te.Detail.FieldPath, e.Detail.Constraint))\n\t\t}\n\tdefault:\n\t\t// For other error types, just add the message\n\t\tif e.Message != \"\" {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", e.Message))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn sb.String()\n}\n\n// Unwrap returns the underlying error.\nfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) Unwrap() error {\n\treturn e.UnderlyingErr\n}\n\n// Code returns the error code for programmatic handling.\nfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) Code() ErrorCode {\n\tswitch e.Kind {\n\tcase ParseErrorKindSyntax:\n\t\treturn ErrCodeInvalidSyntax\n\tcase ParseErrorKindStructure:\n\t\tif e.Detail.DuplicateKey != \"\" {\n\t\t\treturn ErrCodeDuplicateKey\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn ErrCodeInvalidStructure\n\tcase ParseErrorKindTypeMismatch:\n\t\treturn ErrCodeTypeMismatch\n\tcase ParseErrorKindIO:\n\t\treturn ErrCodeFileIOError\n\tcase ParseErrorKindValidation:\n\t\treturn ErrCodeValidationFailed\n\tcase ParseErrorKindSchema:\n\t\treturn ErrCodeSchemaValidation\n\tcase ParseErrorKindEmpty:\n\t\treturn ErrCodeFileEmpty\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn ErrCodeParseError\n\t}\n}\n\n// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n\tswitch e.Kind {\n\tcase ParseErrorKindSyntax:\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeSyntax\n\tcase ParseErrorKindStructure:\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeStructure\n\tcase ParseErrorKindTypeMismatch:\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeTypeMismatch\n\tcase ParseErrorKindIO:\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeIO\n\tcase ParseErrorKindValidation:\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeValidation\n\tcase ParseErrorKindSchema:\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeSchema\n\tcase ParseErrorKindEmpty:\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeEmpty\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeUnknown\n\t}\n}\n\n// Context implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) Context() string {\n\tvar sb strings.Builder\n\n\tif e.LocationInfo.Line > 0 {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"line: %d\", e.LocationInfo.Line))\n\t\tif e.LocationInfo.Column > 0 {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", column: %d\", e.LocationInfo.Column))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif e.Detail.FieldPath != \"\" {\n\t\tif sb.Len() > 0 {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\", \")\n\t\t}\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"field: %s\", e.Detail.FieldPath))\n\t}\n\n\tif e.Detail.Location != \"\" {\n\t\tif sb.Len() > 0 {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\", \")\n\t\t}\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"location: %s\", e.Detail.Location))\n\t}\n\n\treturn sb.String()\n}\n\n// String returns a detailed multi-line error representation with source snippet.\nfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) String() string {\n\tvar sb strings.Builder\n\n\t// Error header\n\tsb.WriteString(e.Error())\n\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n\n\t// Show snippet with caret pointer if we have it\n\tif e.LocationInfo.Snippet != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n\t\tsb.WriteString(\"  \")  // Add 2-space prefix for consistency\n\t\tsb.WriteString(e.LocationInfo.Snippet)\n\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n\n\t\t// Add column indicator if available\n\t\tif e.LocationInfo.Column > 0 && e.LocationInfo.Column <= len(e.LocationInfo.Snippet) {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"  \")  // Add 2-space prefix for consistency\n\t\t\tfor i := 0; i < e.LocationInfo.Column-1; i++ {\n\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\" \")\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"^--- here\\n\")\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Add surrounding context if available\n\tif len(e.LocationInfo.SurroundingLines) > 0 {\n\t\t// Add blank line separator if we showed the snippet\n\t\tif e.LocationInfo.Snippet != \"\" {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n\t\t}\n\t\tfor i, line := range e.LocationInfo.SurroundingLines {\n\t\t\tif i == e.LocationInfo.SnippetLineIndex {\n\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"> \")\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"  \")\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(line)\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn sb.String()\n}\n\n// IsSyntaxError checks if this is a syntax error.\nfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsSyntaxError() bool {\n\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindSyntax\n}\n\n// IsStructureError checks if this is a structure error.\nfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsStructureError() bool {\n\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindStructure\n}\n\n// IsTypeMismatchError checks if this is a type mismatch error.\nfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsTypeMismatchError() bool {\n\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindTypeMismatch\n}\n\n// IsIOError checks if this is an I/O error.\nfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsIOError() bool {\n\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindIO\n}\n\n// IsValidationError checks if this is a validation error.\nfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsValidationError() bool {\n\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindValidation\n}\n\n// IsSchemaError checks if this is a schema error.\nfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsSchemaError() bool {\n\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindSchema\n}\n\n// IsEmpty checks if this is an empty file error.\nfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsEmpty() bool {\n\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindEmpty\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Error Construction Helpers\n// ============================================================================\n\n// NewSyntaxParseError creates a new syntax error.\nfunc NewSyntaxParseError(filePath, message string, line, column int, expected, found string) *EnhancedParseError {\n\treturn &EnhancedParseError{\n\t\tKind:     ParseErrorKindSyntax,\n\t\tFilePath: filePath,\n\t\tMessage:  message,\n\t\tLocationInfo: ErrorContext{\n\t\t\tLine:   line,\n\t\t\tColumn: column,\n\t\t},\n\t\tDetail: ParseErrorDetail{\n\t\t\tExpected: expected,\n\t\t\tFound:    found,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n}\n\n// NewStructureParseError creates a new structure error.\nfunc NewStructureParseError(filePath, message string, line int, duplicateKey, location string) *EnhancedParseError {\n\treturn &EnhancedParseError{\n\t\tKind:     ParseErrorKindStructure,\n\t\tFilePath: filePath,\n\t\tMessage:  message,\n\t\tLocationInfo: ErrorContext{\n\t\t\tLine: line,\n\t\t},\n\t\tDetail: ParseErrorDetail{\n\t\t\tDuplicateKey: duplicateKey,\n\t\t\tLocation:     location,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n}\n\n// NewTypeMismatchParseError creates a new type mismatch error.\nfunc NewTypeMismatchParseError(filePath, message string, line int, fieldPath, expectedType, actualType, value string) *EnhancedParseError {\n\treturn &EnhancedParseError{\n\t\tKind:     ParseErrorKindTypeMismatch,\n\t\tFilePath: filePath,\n\t\tMessage:  message,\n\t\tLocationInfo: ErrorContext{\n\t\t\tLine: line,\n\t\t},\n\t\tDetail: ParseErrorDetail{\n\t\t\tFieldPath:    fieldPath,\n\t\t\tExpectedType: expectedType,\n\t\t\tActualType:   actualType,\n\t\t\tValue:        value,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n}\n\n// NewIOParseError creates a new I/O error.\nfunc NewIOParseError(filePath, message string, line int, underlyingErr error) *EnhancedParseError {\n\treturn &EnhancedParseError{\n\t\tKind:          ParseErrorKindIO,\n\t\tFilePath:      filePath,\n\t\tMessage:       message,\n\t\tUnderlyingErr: underlyingErr,\n\t\tLocationInfo: ErrorContext{\n\t\t\tLine: line,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n}\n\n// NewValidationParseError creates a new validation error.\nfunc NewValidationParseError(filePath, message string, line int, fieldPath, constraintType, constraint string) *EnhancedParseError {\n\treturn &EnhancedParseError{\n\t\tKind:     ParseErrorKindValidation,\n\t\tFilePath: filePath,\n\t\tMessage:  message,\n\t\tLocationInfo: ErrorContext{\n\t\t\tLine: line,\n\t\t},\n\t\tDetail: ParseErrorDetail{\n\t\t\tFieldPath:      fieldPath,\n\t\t\tConstraintType: constraintType,\n\t\t\tConstraint:     constraint,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n}\n\n// NewSchemaParseError creates a new schema error.\nfunc NewSchemaParseError(filePath, message string, line int, schemaPath, schemaName string) *EnhancedParseError {\n\treturn &EnhancedParseError{\n\t\tKind:     ParseErrorKindSchema,\n\t\tFilePath: filePath,\n\t\tMessage:  message,\n\t\tLocationInfo: ErrorContext{\n\t\t\tLine: line,\n\t\t},\n\t\tDetail: ParseErrorDetail{\n\t\t\tSchemaPath: schemaPath,\n\t\t\tSchemaName: schemaName,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n}\n\n// NewEmptyParseError creates a new empty file error.\nfunc NewEmptyParseError(filePath string) *EnhancedParseError {\n\treturn &EnhancedParseError{\n\t\tKind:     ParseErrorKindEmpty,\n\t\tFilePath: filePath,\n\t\tMessage:  \"file is empty or contains only whitespace\",\n\t}\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Error Propagation Strategy\n// ============================================================================\n\n// The error propagation strategy for ParseError follows these principles:\n//\n// 1. Always provide rich context: Include line numbers, column positions, and\n//    source snippets whenever possible.\n//\n// 2. Preserve error chain: Use error wrapping (errors.Wrap, fmt.Errorf with %w)\n//    to maintain the full error chain for debugging.\n//\n// 3. Type-safe error handling: Use Result[T, ParseError] pattern for compile-time\n//    error handling guarantees.\n//\n// 4. Error transformation: Transform lower-level errors (e.g., yaml.v3 errors)\n//    into EnhancedParseError with appropriate context and snippets.\n//\n// 5. Fail-fast on critical errors: I/O errors and syntax errors should immediately\n//    return errors without attempting further processing.\n//\n// Error Flow:\n//   Raw Error → EnhancedParseError (with context) → Result[T, ParseError]\n//\n// Example propagation:\n//   yaml.Unmarshal error → extract line/column → read source snippet →\n//   NewSyntaxParseError with snippet → Result[T, ParseError]\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Integration with Legacy Error Types\n// ============================================================================\n\n// ToLegacySyntaxError converts EnhancedParseError to legacy SyntaxError.\nfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) ToLegacySyntaxError() *SyntaxError {\n\tif e.Kind != ParseErrorKindSyntax {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\treturn &SyntaxError{\n\t\tFilePath:  e.FilePath,\n\t\tLine:      e.LocationInfo.Line,\n\t\tColumn:    e.LocationInfo.Column,\n\t\tMessage:   e.Message,\n\t\tExpected:  e.Detail.Expected,\n\t\tFound:     e.Detail.Found,\n\t\tErr:       e.UnderlyingErr,\n\t\tErrorCode: e.Code(),\n\t}\n}\n\n// ToLegacyStructureError converts EnhancedParseError to legacy StructureError.\nfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) ToLegacyStructureError() *StructureError {\n\tif e.Kind != ParseErrorKindStructure {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\treturn &StructureError{\n\t\tFilePath:     e.FilePath,\n\t\tLine:         e.LocationInfo.Line,\n\t\tMessage:      e.Message,\n\t\tDuplicateKey: e.Detail.DuplicateKey,\n\t\tLocation:     e.Detail.Location,\n\t\tErr:          e.UnderlyingErr,\n\t\tErrorCode:    e.Code(),\n\t}\n}\n\n// ToLegacyTypeMismatchError converts EnhancedParseError to legacy TypeMismatchError.\nfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) ToLegacyTypeMismatchError() *TypeMismatchError {\n\tif e.Kind != ParseErrorKindTypeMismatch {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\treturn &TypeMismatchError{\n\t\tFilePath:     e.FilePath,\n\t\tFieldPath:    e.Detail.FieldPath,\n\t\tExpectedType: e.Detail.ExpectedType,\n\t\tActualType:   e.Detail.ActualType,\n\t\tValue:        e.Detail.Value,\n\t\tLine:         e.LocationInfo.Line,\n\t\tErrorCode:    e.Code(),\n\t}\n}\n\n// ToLegacyParseError converts EnhancedParseError to legacy ParseError.\nfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) ToLegacyParseError() *ParseError {\n\treturn &ParseError{\n\t\tFilePath:   e.FilePath,\n\t\tLine:       e.LocationInfo.Line,\n\t\tColumn:     e.LocationInfo.Column,\n\t\tMessage:    e.Message,\n\t\tContextStr: e.Context(),\n\t\tErr:        e.UnderlyingErr,\n\t\tErrorType:  e.YAMLErrorType(),\n\t\tErrorCode:  e.Code(),\n\t}\n}\n","numLines":540,"startLine":1,"totalLines":540}}}
